message.content:
One gap remains: this is still orchestrating Zellij through shell calls, not yet through a richer internal
  control layer, so features like tab deduping/focus-if-open, attention indicators, and more advanced pane
  targeting are not implemented yet. Those would be the next serious step if you want dellij to feel fully
  authoritative.

content.content:
1→# dellij 2→ 3→

4→ 5→

6→ 7→**dellij** is a Rust-first terminal workspace manager for **Zellij**. 8→It combines Superset-style worktree isolation with cmux-style terminal orchestration and keeps the existing Rust WASM status plugin. 9→ 10→[https://github.com/Diogenesoftoronto/dellij](https://github.com/Diogenesoftoronto/dellij) 11→ 12→## What Was Borrowed 13→ 14→From Superset: 15→ 16→- isolated git workspaces 17→- setup and teardown automation 18→- persistent workspace metadata for ports, URLs, and launch context 19→ 20→From cmux: 21→ 22→- terminal-first orchestration 23→- generated multiplexer layouts 24→- lightweight status surfaces 25→- explicit lifecycle commands 26→ 27→## Workspace Layout 28→ 29→```text 30→. 31→├── Cargo.toml 32→├── crates/ 33→│ └── dellij/ # Rust CLI 34→└── plugin/ # Rust Zellij WASM plugin 35→``` 36→ 37→## Features 38→ 39→- Rust CLI for `open`, `new`, `run`, `bookmark`, `list`, `show`, `status`, `close`, `layout`, and `doctor` 40→- Git-worktree workspace creation with tracked prompt, agent, branch, ports, URLs, notes, and status 41→- `dellij` owns session creation, attach, and workspace-tab opening so users do not need raw `zellij` commands 42→- Reusable bookmarks and workspace task runners inspired by `zellij-bookmarks`, `zellij-playbooks`, and floating-pane tools like `ghost` 43→- `workspace_setup` and `workspace_teardown` hooks in `.dellij/hooks/` 44→- Status snapshots in `.dellij/status/*.json` for the plugin 45→- Generated Zellij KDL layouts with agent, shell, and git-status panes 46→ 47→## Install 48→ 49→```bash 50→cargo install --path crates/dellij 51→cargo build -p dellij-status --release --target wasm32-wasip1 52→``` 53→ 54→`zellij` is still a required dependency, but it should be invisible in normal use. `dellij` is the interface. 55→ 56→For local development inside this repo: 57→ 58→```bash 59→./dellij init 60→./dellij open 61→``` 62→ 63→## Usage 64→ 65→```bash 66→dellij init 67→dellij open 68→dellij new "add retry metrics to sync service" --agent codex --setup --open --port 3000 --url http://localhost:3000 69→dellij bookmark add test "cargo test -q" --description "fast local test pass" 70→dellij run add-retry-metrics-to-sync-service-codex test --floating 71→dellij list 72→dellij show add-retry-metrics-to-sync-service-codex 73→dellij status add-retry-metrics-to-sync-service-codex review --note "ready for review" 74→``` 75→ 76→For a fuller walkthrough, see [GUIDE.md](./GUIDE.md). 77→ 78→## Development 79→ 80→This project uses [mise](https://mise.jdx.sh/) for a small Rust-centric task surface: 81→ 82→- `mise run setup` 83→- `mise run build` 84→- `mise run build:plugin` 85→- `mise run dev` 86→- `mise run release` 87→ 88→## License 89→ 90→MIT 91→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1→# Dellij Guide
     2→
     3→This guide covers the Rust CLI workflow.
     4→
     5→## What Dellij Does
     6→
     7→`dellij` keeps workspace state in `.dellij/`, creates git worktrees, writes status snapshots for the Zellij plugin, generates Zellij layouts for each workspace, and opens or attaches sessions on your behalf.
     8→
     9→## Prerequisites
    10→
    11→You need:
    12→
    13→- `git`
    14→- `zellij`
    15→- Rust toolchain
    16→- at least one agent CLI if you want generated launch commands to be runnable
    17→
    18→You should not need to call `zellij` directly in day-to-day use.
    19→
    20→Common agent IDs include:
    21→
    22→- `codex`
    23→- `claude`
    24→- `aider`
    25→
    26→## Install Dellij
    27→
    28→```bash
    29→cd /path/to/dellij
    30→cargo install --path crates/dellij
    31→```
    32→
    33→Build the plugin:
    34→
    35→```bash
    36→cargo build -p dellij-status --release --target wasm32-wasip1
    37→```
    38→
    39→## First Run
    40→
    41→From the repository you want to manage:
    42→
    43→```bash
    44→dellij init
    45→dellij open
    46→```
    47→
    48→This creates:
    49→
    50→- `.dellij/dellij.json`
    51→- `.dellij/hooks/`
    52→- `.dellij/layouts/`
    53→- `.dellij/status/`
    54→- `.dellij/workspaces/`
    55→
    56→## Basic Workflow
    57→
    58→### 1. Open the project session
    59→
    60→```bash
    61→dellij open
    62→```
    63→
    64→This creates or attaches to the project session managed by `dellij`.
    65→
    66→### 2. Create and open a workspace
    67→
    68→```bash
    69→dellij new "add pagination to the admin API" --agent codex --setup --open
    70→```
    71→
    72→This creates a git worktree in `.dellij/workspaces/<slug>`, creates a branch, stores metadata in `.dellij/dellij.json`, writes `.dellij/status/<slug>.json`, optionally runs `.dellij/hooks/workspace_setup`, and opens the workspace tab for you.
    73→
    74→### 3. Re-open an existing workspace
    75→
    76→```bash
    77→dellij open add-pagination-to-the-admin-api-codex
    78→```
    79→
    80→### 4. Save reusable commands
    81→
    82→```bash
    83→dellij bookmark add test "cargo test -q" --description "fast local test pass"
    84→dellij bookmark add dev "cargo run"
    85→```
    86→
    87→This borrows from the Zellij bookmarks and playbooks ecosystem, but keeps the workflow in `dellij`.
    88→
    89→### 5. Run a command in a workspace
    90→
    91→```bash
    92→dellij run add-pagination-to-the-admin-api-codex test --floating
    93→```
    94→
    95→Inside Zellij, this opens a new pane for the command. Outside Zellij, it runs the command in the workspace directory.
    96→
    97→### 6. Inspect a workspace
    98→
    99→```bash
   100→dellij show add-pagination-to-the-admin-api-codex
   101→```
   102→
   103→### 7. Update status
   104→
   105→```bash
   106→dellij status add-pagination-to-the-admin-api-codex review --note "ready for review"
   107→```
   108→
   109→### 8. Close a workspace
   110→
   111→```bash
   112→dellij close add-pagination-to-the-admin-api-codex --teardown
   113→```
   114→
   115→## Hooks
   116→
   117→The Rust CLI loads executable hooks from `.dellij/hooks/`.
   118→
   119→Available hooks:
   120→
   121→- `workspace_setup`
   122→- `workspace_teardown`
   123→
   124→Example:
   125→
   126→```bash
   127→mkdir -p .dellij/hooks
   128→cat > .dellij/hooks/workspace_setup <<'EOF'
   129→#!/usr/bin/env bash
   130→set -euo pipefail
   131→echo "Preparing $DELLIJ_SLUG in $DELLIJ_WORKTREE_PATH"
   132→EOF
   133→chmod +x .dellij/hooks/workspace_setup
   134→```
   135→
   136→## Feature Mapping
   137→
   138→Superset ideas carried over:
   139→
   140→- isolated git workspaces
   141→- workspace lifecycle scripts
   142→- durable metadata for ports and URLs
   143→- per-workspace launch context
   144→
   145→cmux ideas carried over:
   146→
   147→- terminal-first orchestration
   148→- layout generation
   149→- status files for live multiplexer surfaces
   150→- explicit CLI lifecycle commands
   151→
   152→Ideas pulled from the wider Zellij plugin ecosystem:
   153→
   154→- session-switcher style project/session opening through `dellij open`
   155→- bookmark and playbook style task launching through `dellij bookmark` and `dellij run`
   156→- floating helper panes for one-off tasks
   157→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# Build the dellij-status zellij plugin
     3→# Requires: Rust toolchain with wasm32-wasip1 target
     4→# Install target: rustup target add wasm32-wasip1
     5→
     6→set -euo pipefail
     7→
     8→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
     9→cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
    10→
    11→echo "Adding wasm32-wasip1 target..."
    12→rustup target add wasm32-wasip1
    13→
    14→echo "Building dellij-status plugin..."
    15→cargo build --release --target wasm32-wasip1
    16→
    17→WASM_PATH="$SCRIPT_DIR/target/wasm32-wasip1/release/dellij_status.wasm"
    18→
    19→if [ -f "$WASM_PATH" ]; then
    20→  echo ""
    21→  echo "Plugin built successfully: $WASM_PATH"
    22→  echo ""
    23→  echo "To install globally:"
    24→  echo "  mkdir -p ~/.config/zellij/plugins"

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me create a detailed summary:

## Comprehensive Overview of dellij Project

I've thoroughly explored the dellij project at `/home/diogenes/Projects/dellij`. Here's a comprehensive breakdown:

---

### 1. **Project Purpose**

**dellij** is a Rust-first terminal workspace manager for Zellij that combines:
- **Superset** concepts: isolated git worktrees, setup/teardown automation, persistent workspace metadata
- **cmux** concepts: terminal-first orchestration, generated layouts, lightweight status surfaces, explicit lifecycle commands
- **Zellij plugin ecosystem**: session switching, bookmarks/playbooks for task launching, floating panes

It enables developers to create isolated git-based workspaces with AI agent integration, automatic branching, and metadata tracking—all orchestrated through Zellij.

---

### 2. **Overall Architecture**

The project has a **workspace structure**:
```
/home/diogenes/Projects/dellij/
├── Cargo.toml                          # Workspace root (members: crates/dellij, plugin)
├── crates/dellij/                      # Main Rust CLI binary
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   └── src/main.rs                     # 910 lines: single monolithic file
├── plugin/                             # Zellij WASM plugin
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── build.sh                        # Build script for WASM plugin
│   └── src/main.rs                     # 220 lines: Zellij plugin
├── mise.toml                           # Task runner configuration
├── README.md                           # Project documentation
├── GUIDE.md                            # Usage guide
└── .dellij/                            # Project state directory (created by dellij init)
    ├── dellij.config.json              # Project configuration
    ├── hooks/                          # Optional workspace lifecycle hooks
    ├── status/                         # Status snapshots read by plugin
    ├── layouts/                        # Generated KDL layouts per workspace
    └── workspaces/                     # Git worktrees
```

---

### 3. **How It Orchestrates Zellij: Shell Calls vs Plugin API**

**dellij uses a hybrid approach:**

**CLI (Shell/Process-based):**
- Communicates with Zellij via subprocess calls to `zellij` binary
- Uses `zellij` command-line interface to:
  - Create/attach sessions: `zellij --session <name>`
  - Manage tabs: `zellij action new-tab --layout <path>`
  - List sessions: `zellij list-sessions`
  - Run commands in panes: `zellij run --cwd --name --floating`

**Plugin (Zellij API):**
- The Rust WASM plugin (`dellij-status`) uses `zellij_tile` crate (v0.43.1)
- Subscribes to Zellij events: `Timer`, `TabUpdate`, `PermissionRequestResult`
- Requests permissions: `ReadApplicationState`
- Renders a status ribbon in Zellij UI showing workspace statuses
- **Does NOT** use the full plugin API for orchestration—only for UI status display

**State Communication:**
- CLI writes JSON status files to `.dellij/status/<slug>.json`
- Plugin reads these files every 2 seconds (Timer event)
- Plugin displays agent status with color-coded dots (working=yellow, waiting=cyan, error=red, done=green)

---

### 4. **Main Entry Points**

**Primary Binary:** `/home/diogenes/Projects/dellij/crates/dellij/src/main.rs`
- Compiled to `dellij` CLI executable
- Installed via: `cargo install --path crates/dellij`
- Entry point: `fn main()` → parses CLI args → loads/initializes `App` → runs command

**Plugin:** `/home/diogenes/Projects/dellij/plugin/src/main.rs`
- Compiled to WASM: `cargo build -p dellij-status --release --target wasm32-wasip1`
- Produces: `plugin/target/wasm32-wasip1/release/dellij_status.wasm`
- Installed to: `~/.config/zellij/plugins/dellij_status.wasm` (or used from project dir)

---

### 5. **CLI Commands and Their Implementations**

All implemented in `/home/diogenes/Projects/dellij/crates/dellij/src/main.rs`:

| Command | Function | Behavior |
|---------|----------|----------|
| `dellij init` | `App::init()` | Creates `.dellij/` directory structure, ensures Zellij is available |
| `dellij open [slug]` | `App::open(args)` | Opens project session or specific workspace. Can create with `--create` flag |
| `dellij new <prompt>` | `App::new_workspace()` | Creates git worktree, generates workspace metadata, optionally runs setup hook |
| `dellij list` | `App::list()` | Displays all tracked workspaces with slug, status, agent, ports, path |
| `dellij show <slug>` | `App::show()` | Prints workspace JSON details |
| `dellij run <slug> <target>` | `App::run_in_workspace()` | Executes bookmark or raw command in workspace (floating pane if inside Zellij) |
| `dellij bookmark add <name> <cmd>` | `App::bookmark()` | Saves reusable command bookmarks |
| `dellij bookmark list` | `App::bookmark()` | Lists all bookmarks |
| `dellij bookmark remove <name>` | `App::bookmark()` | Removes a bookmark |
| `dellij status <slug> <state>` | `App::update_status()` | Updates workspace status (working/waiting/blocked/review/done/error) |
| `dellij close <slug>` | `App::close()` | Removes worktree, teardown hook, and workspace metadata |
| `dellij layout <slug>` | `App::print_layout()` | Outputs generated KDL layout for a workspace |
| `dellij doctor` | `App::doctor()` | Diagnostic: checks git, zellij, hooks dir, counts workspaces/bookmarks |

---

### 6. **Existing Plugin Code and Zellij APIs Used**

**File:** `/home/diogenes/Projects/dellij/plugin/src/main.rs` (220 lines)

**Structure:**
```rust
struct DellijStatusPlugin {
    agents: Vec<AgentInfo>,        // Parsed from status files
    config_dir: String,             // Path to .dellij/
    tick: u64,                      // Timer counter
    active_tab: Option<String>,     // Current active tab name
}

struct AgentInfo {
    slug: String,                   // Workspace identifier
    short_label: String,            // 2-letter abbreviation (cc, oc, cx, etc.)
    status: String,                 // working/waiting/error/done
}
```

**Zellij API Usage:**
1. **Initialization (`load`):**
   - `set_selectable(false)` — Plugin is read-only status ribbon
   - `request_permission(&[PermissionType::ReadApplicationState])` — Reads workspace state
   - `subscribe(&[EventType::Timer, EventType::TabUpdate, EventType::PermissionRequestResult])`
   - `set_timeout(2.0)` — Poll every 2 seconds

2. **Event Handling (`update`):**
   - `Timer` events: Reads status JSON files, rerenders
   - `TabUpdate` events: Tracks active tab
   - `PermissionRequestResult`: Confirms permissions granted

3. **Rendering (`render`):**
   - Uses `zellij_tile::ui_components::{print_ribbon, Text}`
   - Builds colored text: `" dellij cc ● working oc ● error "`
   - Color indices: 0=primary, 1=secondary, 2=green, 3=yellow, 4=red, 7=cyan
   - Range-based coloring for agent segments and status dots

**Agent Label Mapping:**
Maps workspace slugs to 2-letter abbreviations:
- `claude-code` → `cc`
- `opencode` → `oc`
- `codex` → `cx`
- `cline` → `cl`
- `gemini` → `gm`
- (+ 8 more known agents; falls back to first 2 chars)

---

### 7. **Key Files and Their Purposes**

| File | Purpose | Size |
|------|---------|------|
| `/crates/dellij/src/main.rs` | Main CLI implementation: commands, workspace lifecycle, git interaction, Zellij orchestration | 910 lines |
| `/plugin/src/main.rs` | Zellij WASM plugin: status ribbon UI, file polling, color rendering | 220 lines |
| `/plugin/build.sh` | Helper script to build WASM and provide install instructions | 32 lines |
| `/.dellij/dellij.config.json` | Project configuration (legacy format, JSON) | Dynamic |
| `/.dellij/status/<slug>.json` | Per-workspace status snapshot for plugin (written by CLI) | Per-workspace |
| `/.dellij/layouts/<slug>.kdl` | Generated Zellij KDL layout (3 panes: agent cmd, shell, git status) | Per-workspace |
| `/.dellij/hooks/workspace_setup` | Optional bash script, runs on `dellij new --setup` | User-defined |
| `/.dellij/hooks/workspace_teardown` | Optional bash script, runs on `dellij close --teardown` | User-defined |
| `/crates/dellij/Cargo.toml` | CLI dependencies: anyhow, camino, chrono, clap, serde, serde_json | Simple manifest |
| `/plugin/Cargo.toml` | Plugin dependencies: zellij-tile 0.43.1, serde, serde_json | Minimal |
| `/Cargo.toml` | Workspace root (members + shared versions) | Manifest |
| `mise.toml` | Build tasks (setup, build, build:plugin, dev, release) | Task config |
| `/README.md` | High-level overview, features, install instructions | 91 lines |
| `/GUIDE.md` | Detailed usage walkthrough with examples | 157 lines |

---

### 8. **Key Data Structures and Workflows**

**Main `App` struct** (lines 140-174):
- Holds project root, state dir, config path, and loaded `Config`
- Loads/initializes from `.dellij/dellij.json`
- All commands dispatch through `app.run(command)`

**`Config` struct** (lines 683-702):
- `project_root`, `created_at`, `settings`, `bookmarks`, `workspaces`
- Persisted to `dellij.json` as JSON

**`Workspace` struct** (lines 730-745):
- Complete metadata: slug, prompt, agent, branch, worktree path, status, ports, URLs, notes
- Serialized to `dellij.json` and written as snapshot to `status/<slug>.json`

**Workspace Lifecycle:**
1. **Create**: `dellij new <prompt> --agent <name>`
   - Generate slug via `slugify(prompt, agent)`
   - Create git worktree: `git worktree add -b <branch> <path> <base_branch>`
   - Write workspace to config
   - Write status file for plugin
   - Generate KDL layout with 3 panes
   - Optionally run `workspace_setup` hook
   
2. **Open**: `dellij open <slug>`
   - Ensure session exists: `zellij --session <name>` or `zellij attach`
   - Create tab with layout: `zellij action new-tab --layout <path>`
   
3. **Run**: `dellij run <slug> <bookmark/cmd>`
   - If inside Zellij: `zellij run --floating --cwd <path> -- bash -lc <cmd>`
   - If outside: Run directly with `bash -lc <cmd>`
   
4. **Status**: `dellij status <slug> <state> --note <text>`
   - Update workspace status field
   - Append to notes array
   - Rewrite status file (plugin polls this)
   
5. **Close**: `dellij close <slug> --teardown`
   - Optionally run `workspace_teardown` hook
   - Remove git worktree: `git worktree remove --force <path>`
   - Delete status file
   - Remove from config

---

### 9. **External Dependencies**

**Workspace:**
```toml
anyhow = "1.0"               # Error handling
camino = "1.1"               # UTF-8 paths
chrono = "0.4"               # Timestamps
clap = "4.5"                 # CLI parsing
serde = "1.0"                # Serialization
serde_json = "1.0"           # JSON support
thiserror = "2.0"            # Error derivation
```

**Plugin:**
```toml
zellij-tile = "0.43.1"       # Zellij plugin API
serde, serde_json            # JSON handling
```

**External Tools:**
- `git` — Worktree and branch management
- `zellij` — Session, tab, and pane orchestration
- Agent CLIs (codex, claude, aider) — Rendered into launch commands

---

### 10. **Notable Implementation Details**

1. **Zellij Detection**: `inside_zellij()` checks for `ZELLIJ` env var
2. **Project Root Detection**: Tries `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, falls back to CWD
3. **Shell Escaping**: Custom `shell_escape()` for rendering agent commands in bash
4. **Status Enumeration**: `WorkspaceStatus` with 6 states (working, waiting, blocked, review, done, error)
5. **KDL Layout Generation**: Hard-coded 3-pane layout (agent cmd, shell, git status)
6. **Hook Execution**: Sets 7 env vars (DELLIJ_ROOT, DELLIJ_SLUG, etc.) before running hooks
7. **Bookmark Resolution**: `run_in_workspace()` resolves bookmark by name or uses raw command
8. **Session Naming**: Format: `dellij-<hash>` where hash is slugified project root

---

This is a well-architected Rust project that cleanly separates CLI orchestration (shell-based) from plugin UI display (Zellij API-based), with persistent state stored in JSON and stateless layout generation for reproducible workspace setup.

chrono = "0.4"               # Timestamps
clap = "4.5"                 # CLI parsing
serde = "1.0"                # Serialization
serde_json = "1.0"           # JSON support
thiserror = "2.0"            # Error derivation
```

**Plugin:**
```toml
zellij-tile = "0.43.1"       # Zellij plugin API
serde, serde_json            # JSON handling
```

**External Tools:**
- `git` — Worktree and branch management
- `zellij` — Session, tab, and pane orchestration
- Agent CLIs (codex, claude, aider) — Rendered into launch commands

---

### 10. **Notable Implementation Details**

1. **Zellij Detection**: `inside_zellij()` checks for `ZELLIJ` env var
2. **Project Root Detection**: Tries `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, falls back to CWD
3. **Shell Escaping**: Custom `shell_escape()` for rendering agent commands in bash
4. **Status Enumeration**: `WorkspaceStatus` with 6 states (working, waiting, blocked, review, done, error)
5. **KDL Layout Generation**: Hard-coded 3-pane layout (agent cmd, shell, git status)
6. **Hook Execution**: Sets 7 env vars (DELLIJ_ROOT, DELLIJ_SLUG, etc.) before running hooks
7. **Bookmark Resolution**: `run_in_workspace()` resolves bookmark by name or uses raw command
8. **Session Naming**: Format: `dellij-<hash>` where hash is slugified project root

---

This is a well-architected Rust project that cleanly separates CLI orchestration (shell-based) from plugin UI display (Zellij API-based), with persistent state stored in JSON and stateless layout generation for reproducible workspace setup.

The gap is real and well-defined. The three features you mentioned map to specific Zellij plugin API capabilities that are currently unused. Here's what the control layer upgrade would look like:

---

## The Core Mechanism: `zellij pipe`

Zellij 0.43+ has a pipe API that lets the CLI send JSON messages to a running plugin:

```bash
zellij pipe --name dellij --plugin file:<path> -- '{"action":"open","slug":"my-workspace"}'
```

The plugin receives this as a `PipeMessage` event and can respond using the full plugin API — no more fire-and-forget shell calls.

---

## What Each Feature Requires

### Tab deduping / focus-if-open
The plugin already subscribes to `TabUpdate`. We need to:
1. Expand it to track **all** tabs (`HashMap<usize, String>` of pos → name), not just the active one
2. Subscribe to `PipeMessage` events
3. On `open` message: check if a tab named `dellij-<slug>` exists → `switch_tab_to(pos)` vs. `new_tab_with_layout()`
4. Replace the CLI's `zellij action new-tab --layout` shell call with a pipe message

### Attention indicators
The plugin already polls status files and knows `status == "blocked" | "error"`. What's missing:
- Knowing which tabs are **not** currently focused
- Rendering non-active tabs with error/blocked status differently (e.g., a `!` glyph or red ribbon segment)
- This is pure rendering logic once tab tracking is in place — no new Zellij API needed

### Advanced pane targeting
- Subscribe to `PaneUpdate` to get `PaneId`s per tab
- On `run` message: `focus_terminal_pane(pane_id, ...)` + write command via `write_chars()` or `write_to_stdin()`
- Enables targeting the "shell" pane vs. the "agent" pane within a workspace tab, rather than always floating

---

## Structural Change Required

Right now the plugin is **read-only** (status display). This upgrade makes it **read-write** (also controls Zellij state). That means:

1. The plugin needs `WriteToStdin` and `ChangeApplicationState` permissions in addition to `ReadApplicationState`
2. The CLI's `open()` and `run()` methods need to detect if the plugin is already running and prefer pipe over shell fallback
3. The plugin needs a "not running yet" bootstrap path (first open still goes through shell, which loads the plugin)

---

## Scope estimate

| Feature | Plugin changes | CLI changes | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tab dedup/focus | Track all tabs, handle PipeMessage, switch_tab_to | Replace `new-tab` shell call with pipe | Low |
| Attention indicators | Render non-active error/blocked tabs distinctly | None | Trivial (once tab tracking is done) |
| Pane targeting | Track PaneUpdate, focus_terminal_pane, write_chars | Replace floating `zellij run` with pipe | Medium |

Tab dedup is the highest-value / lowest-risk starting point — want me to implement that first?

input.description: Fetch awesome-zellij README via gh CLI

content.content:
# Awesome [Zellij](https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij)

A list of resources for Zellij workspace: plugins, tutorials and configuration settings.

All the resources listed are community-driven: we cannot offer support but suggestions and comments are very welcome.

# Plugins

## Navigation

* [harpoon](https://github.com/Nacho114/harpoon) quickly navigate panes (clone of nvim's harpoon)
* [room](https://github.com/rvcas/room) quickly search and switch tabs 🖤
* [vim-zellij-navigator](https://github.com/hiasr/vim-zellij-navigator) Seamless navigation with vim in zellij
* [zellij-jump-list](https://github.com/blank2121/zellij-jump-list) navigate your motions from pane-to-pane (similar to Vim, Neovim, and Emacs jump list)
* [zellij-nvim-nav-plugin](https://github.com/sharph/zellij-nvim-nav-plugin) Another plugin for seamless navigation with neovim/vim windows
* [zellij-pane-picker](https://github.com/shihanng/zellij-pane-picker) quickly switch, star, and jump to panes with customizable keyboard shortcuts
* [zjpane](https://github.com/FuriouZz/zjpane) Navigate between zellij panes easily

## Session Management

* [zbuffers](https://github.com/Strech/zbuffers) a minimal and convenient way to switch between tabs, inspired by Emacs vertico-buffers and Zellij session-manager
* [zellij-attention](https://github.com/KiryuuLight/zellij-attention) add notification icons to tab names when panes need attention, designed for Claude Code
* [zellij-autolock](https://github.com/fresh2dev/zellij-autolock) Automatically lock Zellij depending on the command in the focused pane. Seamless navigation for Vim and more. Pairs well with [zellij.vim](https://github.com/fresh2dev/zellij.vim).
* [zellij-bookmarks](https://github.com/yaroslavborbat/zellij-bookmarks) manage command bookmarks and quickly insert them into the terminal
* [zellij-cb](https://github.com/ndavd/zellij-cb) a customizable compact bar for Zellij
* [zellij-choose-tree](https://github.com/laperlej/zellij-choose-tree) quickly switch between sessions, inspired by tmux choose-tree
* [zellij-favs](https://github.com/JoseMM2002/zellij-favs) adds a way to save favorites sessions and flush the others
* [zellij-sessionizer](https://github.com/laperlej/zellij-sessionizer) create sessions based on folder names
* [zellij-switch](https://github.com/mostafaqanbaryan/zellij-switch) switching between sessions in CLI using `zellij pipe`
* [zellij-worktree](https://github.com/sharph/zellij-worktree) A Zellij plugin for managing git worktrees
* [zsm](https://github.com/liam-mackie/zsm) A zoxide-integrated session switcher with support for default layouts

## Status Bar 

* [zellaude](https://github.com/ishefi/zellaude) a status bar plugin that shows Claude Code activity indicators on tabs
* [zellij-cb](https://github.com/ndavd/zellij-cb) a customizable compact bar for Zellij
* [zellij-datetime](https://github.com/h1romas4/zellij-datetime) adds a date and time pane to your Zellij
* [zellij-load](https://github.com/Christian-Prather/zellij-load) show system resources such as CPU, memory and GPU usage. Similar to [tmux cpu-usage](https://github.com/dracula/tmux/blob/master/docs/CONFIG.md#cpu-usage---up)
* [zellij-what-time](https://github.com/pirafrank/zellij-what-time) shows host system date and/or time in the status bar. Inspired by zellij-datetime
* [zj-status-bar](https://github.com/cristiand391/zj-status-bar) an opinionated fork of the compact-bar plugin
* [zjstatus](https://github.com/dj95/zjstatus) a configurable, themeable statusbar plugin
* [zjstatus-hints](https://github.com/b0o/zjstatus-hints) adds mode-aware key binding hints to zjstatus

## UI & Modes

* [zellij-autolock](https://github.com/fresh2dev/zellij-autolock) Automatically lock Zellij depending on the command in the focused pane, seamless navigation for Vim, pairs well with [zellij.vim](https://github.com/fresh2dev/zellij.vim).
* [zellij-forgot](https://github.com/karimould/zellij-forgot) swiftly present and access your keybinds (and more)
* [zellij-getmode](https://github.com/chardskarth/zellij-getmode) a simple utility plugin that gets the current input mode of zellij
* [zellij-layoutswitch](https://github.com/sgtrusty/zellij-layoutswitch) switch between layouts and tab panes natively & efficiently without shell bloat
* [zellij-newtab-plus](https://github.com/AlexZasorin/zellij-newtab-plus) create named tabs and navigate using zoxide in one keybind
* [zellij-tab-bar-indexed](https://github.com/ivoronin/zellij-tab-bar-indexed) a tab-bar plugin that adds numeric indices to tabs for quick navigation
* [zellij-vertical-tabs](https://github.com/cfal/zellij-vertical-tabs) a plugin that displays tabs vertically as rows
* [zellij-workspace](https://github.com/vdbulcke/zellij-workspace) apply layouts to current session
* [zjswitcher](https://github.com/WingsZeng/zjswitcher) automatically switch between normal mode and locked mode

## Search

* [grab](https://github.com/imsnif/grab) A fuzzy finder (files, structs, enums, functions) for Rust devs
* [monocole](https://github.com/imsnif/monocle) fuzzy find of file names and contents

## Utilities

* [ghost](https://github.com/vdbulcke/ghost) spawn floating command terminal pane (interactive zrf)
* [multitask](https://github.com/imsnif/multitask) a mini-CI as a Zellij plugin
* [zellij-attention](https://github.com/KiryuuLight/zellij-attention) add notification icons to tab names when panes need attention, designed for Claude Code
* [zellij-bookmarks](https://github.com/yaroslavborbat/zellij-bookmarks) manage command bookmarks and quickly insert them into the terminal
* [zellij-notepad](https://github.com/0xble/zellij-notepad) floating notepad pane with configurable editor, position, and timestamped notes
* [zellij-playbooks](https://github.com/yaroslavborbat/zellij-playbooks) browse, select, and execute commands from playbook files directly in the terminal
* [zellij-qr-share](https://github.com/dbachelder/zellij-qr-share) show a web token as a QR code in the terminal for fast mobile authentication in the web UI.
* [zellij-send-keys](https://github.com/atani/zellij-send-keys) send text/commands to specific panes like tmux send-keys
* [zj-quit](https://github.com/cristiand391/zj-quit) a friendly `quit` plugin for zellij

## External Tools

* [gitpod.zellij](https://github.com/gitpod-samples/gitpod.zellij) Zellij plugin for Gitpod, with .gitpod.yml tasks integration
* [jbz (Just Bacon Zellij)](https://github.com/nim65s/jbz) display your just commands wrapped in bacon
* [zellijira](https://github.com/dam4rus/zellijira) Manage sessions around on Jira issues
* [zellij-theme-configurator](https://rosmur.github.io/zellij-theme-configurator/) A web GUI for creating and exporting Zellij themes
* [zellij-vscode-toolkit](https://github.com/atoolz/zellij-vscode-toolkit) VS Code extension with IntelliSense, validation, hover docs, color preview, and snippets for Zellij config and layout files
* [zj-docker](https://github.com/dj95/zj-docker) display docker containers and perform basic operations
* [zj-git-branch](https://github.com/dam4rus/zj-git-branch) Manage git branches

# Integrations

* [fzf-zellij](https://github.com/k-kuroguro/fzf-zellij) Shell script to start fzf in a Zellij floating pane.
* [opencode-zellij-namer](https://github.com/24601/opencode-zellij-namer) AI-powered dynamic session naming for [OpenCode](https://opencode.ai), automatically renames sessions based on project context
* [theylix](https://codeberg.org/hobgoblina/theylix) Zellij, Helix, and various cli tools ([Yazi](https://github.com/sxyazi/yazi), [Lazygit](https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazygit), [LazySQL](https://github.com/jorgerojas26/lazysql), [Slumber](https://github.com/LucasPickering/slumber), [Serpl](https://github.com/yassinebridi/serpl), `git blame` via [Tig](https://github.com/jonas/tig)) as a zen-mode IDE
* [yazelix](https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix) zellij, yazi and nushell adding a File Tree to Helix & helix-friendly keybindigs for zellij!
* [zeco](https://github.com/julianbuettner/zeco) Share your zellij session over the internet, easy and secure!
* [zellij-sessionizer](https://github.com/victor-falcon/zellij-sessionizer) A fuzzy finder-powered project switcher for Zellij sessions, inspired by [ThePrimeagen/tmux-sessionizer](https://github.com/ThePrimeagen/tmux-sessionizer)
* [zellix](https://github.com/TheEmeraldBee/zellix) A nushell wrapper over helix that leverages the power of zellij to turn it into a plugin system!
* [zide](https://github.com/josephschmitt/zide) Zellij layouts + bash scripts to create an IDE-like file picker and editor workflow that works in any shell and with most any visual file pickers!
* [zrw](https://github.com/ivoronin/zrw) run commands in Zellij panes, wait for completion, and propagate exit codes
  
# Tutorials

## Basics

* [Zellij basic functionality](https://zellij.dev/tutorials/basic-functionality/)
* [Using and creating Layouts](https://zellij.dev/tutorials/layouts/)

## Plugins

* [A comprehensive walkthrough on developing Zellij plugins](https://github.com/Kangaxx-0/first-zellij-plugin)
* [Learnings from developing a zellij plugin](https://blog.nerd.rocks/posts/profiling-zellij-plugins/) an informative blog post from the creator of [zjstatus](https://github.com/dj95/zjstatus)
* [Developing WebAssembly Rust plugins for Zellij](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pgNIcQ8rTXk) Aram Drevekenin's talk at RustLab 2023
* [How to use Zellij Switch plugin](https://mostafaqanbaryan.com/zellij-attach-plugin/) a blog post about using fzf and zoxide with zellij-switch

## Documentation and support

* [The zellij docs](https://zellij.dev/documentation/introduction)
* and of course the zellij [Discord](https://discord.com/invite/CrUAFH3) and [Matrix](https://matrix.to/#/#zellij_general:matrix.org) for questions, support and discussions

Based on the search results, here's what I found about superset.sh:

## Superset - Terminal Workspace Manager for AI Coding Agents

Superset orchestrates CLI-based coding agents across isolated git worktrees, with built-in terminal, review, and open-in-editor workflows.

### Key Features

Run 10+ parallel coding agents on your machine. Isolate each task in its own git worktree so agents don't interfere with each other, monitor all your agents from one place and get notified when they need attention, and review and edit changes quickly with the built-in diff viewer and editor.

### Supported Agents

Use Claude Code, OpenCode, Cursor, or any CLI-based coding tool.

### Core Capabilities

- **Workspace Management**: Workspaces are isolated git worktrees with their own directory, terminal, and ports.

- **Built-in Terminal**: Built-in terminal per workspace with multiple tabs and session persistence.

- **IDE Integration**: Jump into your favorite editor with one click. VS Code, Cursor, Xcode, JetBrains IDEs, or any terminal — open worktrees exactly where you need them.

### Installation

macOS (Apple Silicon or Intel), Git installed, and GitHub CLI authenticated are required. Windows and Linux coming soon.

The tool is designed to help developers run multiple AI coding agents simultaneously without context switching overhead, making it ideal for parallel development workflows, bug fixes, refactors, and feature development.

Fully vibe-coded using CC and Codex. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/mkurman/cmux-windows"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about cmux:

## Overview

cmux is a native macOS app with a GUI: vertical tabs, split panes, an embedded browser, and a socket API are all built in. cmux is a free, open source macOS terminal with split panes, notification rings, and an embedded browser, built for running multiple coding agents.

## Key Features

cmux is scriptable with a CLI and socket API to create workspaces, split panes, send keystrokes, and automate the browser. The sidebar has vertical tabs that show git branch, linked PR status/number, working directory, listening ports, and the latest notification text for each workspace.

It works with Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Kiro, Aider, and any CLI tool.

## Use Cases

cmux excels at allowing a primary agent to spawn and manage multiple "sub-agents," each working in its own dedicated pane. The CLI commands can be broadly categorized: workspace management (list-workspaces, new-workspace, rename-workspace), pane/surface management (new-split, new-pane, close-surface, trigger-flash), interaction commands (send sends text, send-key sends key presses like 'Return'), browser control (a whole suite of browser subcommands for navigation, interaction, and data extraction), and feedback and metadata (notify, set-status, set-progress, list-notifications).

## Technical Details

cmux is built as a native macOS app using Swift and AppKit, not Electron, and is powered by libghostty for smooth rendering. cmux is free to use and the source code is available on GitHub.

content.content:
# Superset Workspaces Features

Based on the documentation, here are the key features:

## Core Functionality
- **Isolation**: "Each workspace = one git branch. Workspaces are isolated git worktrees" with dedicated directories, terminals, and ports
- **Creation options**: New branches, existing branches, or pull requests
- **Worktree import**: Bulk-import existing git worktrees via the Import tab

## Status Tracking
- **Ahead/Behind indicators**: Local workspaces display commit status (↑N for unpushed, ↓N for behind remote)
- **PR status**: Pull request-based workspaces show review status, CI checks, and GitHub links
- **Preview access**: Deployment preview buttons appear when available

## Git Integration
- Own git branch per workspace
- Own working directory
- Workspace-scoped port detection and controls

## Use Cases
- Parallel feature development
- AI agent execution (Claude Code in separate workspaces)
- Code review workflows

## Best Practices
- Descriptive branch naming (e.g., `feature/auth`, `fix/login-bug`)
- Avoid direct main branch work
- Delete workspaces post-merge

## Creation Method
Press **⌘N** or use the "New Workspace" button with prompt-first workflow including PR linking via title, number, or URL.

No information about notifications, orchestration, or specific CLI commands appears in the provided documentation.

# cmux Features Overview

## Core Terminal & Workspace Management
- **Workspaces**: Create, switch, and rename multiple independent workspaces with keyboard shortcuts (⌘N, ⌘1-9)
- **Split Panes**: Divide panes horizontally/vertically (⌘D, ⌘⇧D) with directional focus navigation
- **Vertical Tabs**: Sidebar displays git branch, PR status, working directory, listening ports, and notification text
- **Native macOS App**: Built with Swift/AppKit using libghostty rendering—not Electron

## Notification System
- **Visual Indicators**: Panes receive blue rings and tabs highlight when agents require attention
- **Notification Panel**: Centralized view of all pending notifications with "⌘⇧U" jump-to-latest functionality
- **Terminal Sequences**: Captures OSC 9/99/777 sequences plus `cmux notify` CLI command for agent integration
- **Context-Aware**: Shows meaningful notification text beyond generic "waiting for input" messages

## Browser Integration
- **In-App Browser**: Split browser pane alongside terminal with scriptable automation API
- **Agent-Friendly Controls**: Element reference lookup, accessibility tree snapshots, form filling, JavaScript evaluation
- **Web Navigation**: Standard browser shortcuts (⌘L, ⌘[/], ⌘R) with developer tools support

## Configuration & Compatibility
- **Ghostty Config**: Reads existing `~/.config/ghostty/config` for themes, fonts, colors
- **GPU Acceleration**: Powered by libghostty for smooth terminal rendering
- **Session Restore**: Restores window layout, working directories, scrollback, and browser history on relaunch

## CLI & API
- **Socket API**: Scriptable control for workspaces, panes, keystrokes, browser URLs
- **Command-Line Tool**: `cmux` CLI for programmatic workspace and pane creation
- **Automation Ready**: Integrates with Claude Code, OpenCode, and custom agent hooks

## Additional Features
- **Find**: Full-text search in terminal (⌘F, ⌘G/⌧G)
- **Font Controls**: Zoom terminal (⌘+/-, ⌘0 reset)
- **Multiple Windows**: Create separate cmux instances (⌘⇧N)
- **Settings & Reload**: Persistent configuration with hot-reload (⌘⇧,)

# Superset Terminal Features Overview

Based on the documentation, here are the key terminal capabilities:

## Terminal Features

**Multiple Tabs & Navigation:**
- "⌘T - New tab, ⌘W - Close tab, ⌘1-9 - Switch tabs"
- Independent execution for each tab

**Session Management:**
- "Sessions survive app restarts: Running processes continue, Output history preserved, Scrollback maintained"
- Persistent scrollback buffer across application restarts

**Interactive Capabilities:**
- Double-click pane titles to rename them
- Drag tabs onto split zones to merge panes
- Right-click context menu with Copy, Paste, Mark as Unread, Split, Clear, Move to Tab, and Close options

**Link Handling:**
- Hold Cmd (macOS) or Ctrl and click to open URLs in browser
- File paths open in built-in file viewer by default
- Configurable behavior in Settings → Terminal

**Environment Variables Available:**
- `SUPERSET_ROOT_PATH` - Root repository path
- `SUPERSET_WORKSPACE_NAME` - Current workspace identifier

## Notable Absences

The documentation does not detail CLI commands, agent integration specifics, notifications, status updates, or automation/orchestration features beyond basic terminal functionality.

# Superset CLI Commands

I've reviewed the provided documentation about the Superset monorepo structure, but **the content does not contain a comprehensive list of CLI commands** for the workspace manager.

## What's Available

The documentation references development commands at the root level:

- **`bun dev`** - "Start desktop + API + web + electric-proxy" in parallel
- **`bun dev:all`** - "Start all apps"
- **`bun dev:docs`** - "Start docs site"
- **`bun dev:marketing`** - "Start marketing + docs"
- **`bun build`** - "Build desktop app"
- **`bun test`** - "Run all tests"
- **`bun lint`** - "Run Biome linter"
- **`bun typecheck`** - "Type check all packages"

For desktop-specific operations, there are additional scripts in `apps/desktop/package.json`, but the full CLI reference for the workspace manager itself isn't detailed in this documentation excerpt.

## Recommendation

To find the complete CLI command reference, you would need to check:
1. The root `package.json` scripts section
2. Desktop app documentation (BUILDING.md, RELEASE.md)
3. Potential CLI help output from running `bun --help` or app-specific help commands

The documentation focuses on architecture and deployment rather than end-user CLI documentation.

content.content:
# Superset Features

Based on the website content, here are Superset's key features:

## Core Capabilities
- **Parallel Agent Execution**: "Run dozens of agents at once" across different tasks simultaneously
- **Universal Agent Compatibility**: Works with "any CLI-based coding agent including Claude Code, OpenCode, OpenAI Codex, and more"
- **Git Worktree Isolation**: Each agent operates in its own isolated Git worktree, preventing merge conflicts
- **Multi-IDE Support**: Open workspaces in VS Code, Cursor, Xcode, JetBrains IDEs, or terminal environments

## Workspace Management
- Create and manage multiple parallel workspaces
- Switch between agents and tasks instantly
- Persistent sessions (closing laptop doesn't kill sessions)

## Code Review & Integration
- Built-in diff viewer showing side-by-side or inline changes
- Commit message support and change staging
- Pull request review capabilities
- Deep-linking to external IDEs

## Developer Experience
- Port forwarding functionality
- Terminal integration with multiple concurrent terminals
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) server support
- Real-time agent monitoring and status tracking

## Licensing & Access
- Free tier available
- Open source (Elastic License 2.0)
- Self-hostable with source code inspection available
- Support for personal API keys (no proxying)

# cmux Terminal: Features and Capabilities

## Core Architecture
cmux is a **native macOS application written in Swift using AppKit**, not a tmux clone. It leverages libghostty for terminal emulation, WebKit for browser functionality, and Bonsplit for layout management.

## CLI Commands and Flags

### Workspace Management
- `list-workspaces`
- `new-workspace`
- `rename-workspace`

### Pane/Surface Operations
- `new-split` (with directional options: right, down)
- `new-pane` (supports `--type browser`, `--url` flags)
- `close-surface`
- `read-screen`

### Interaction Commands
- `send` (types text into panes)
- `send-key` (simulates keypresses like Return)
- `trigger-flash` (visual alerts on specific panes)

### Browser Control
Complete suite of `browser` subcommands including:
- `navigate` / `wait` (`--load-state complete`)
- `snapshot` (`--interactive` flag for DOM references)
- `type`, `click` (targeting interactive elements)

### Feedback Systems
- `notify` (`--title`, `--body` flags for macOS notifications)
- `set-status` (sidebar updates with `--icon` for SF Symbols)
- `set-progress` (progress bar display)
- `list-notifications`

## Unique Agent-Centric Features

**Parallel Sub-Agent Orchestration**: "The primary agent can send instructions to each sub-agent, monitor their progress, and gather their results, all within a single, organized cmux workspace."

**DOM Snapshots for Web Interaction**: Browser snapshots return "short, unique references (like e10, e14) to interactive elements," enabling reliable self-correcting agent automation.

**Unix Socket IPC**: Commands communicate via Unix domain socket at `/tmp/cmux.sock` for "nearly instantaneous" low-latency control.

**Claude Code Hooks**: Automatic triggering of scripts on session/task events for seamless notifications without explicit agent requests.

**Sidebar Status Display**: Dynamic ambient awareness showing git branches, test status, build progress, and file names directly in workspace tabs.

## Notable Absence
The article contains no mentions of native MCP integration, git commands, or built-in version control features—these appear to be handled indirectly through agent-executable shell commands rather than dedicated cmux CLI functions.

# cmux Feature List

## Core Terminal Features
- **Native macOS app** built with Swift/AppKit, GPU-accelerated via libghostty
- **Vertical + horizontal tabs** with sidebar showing git branch, PR status, working directory, listening ports
- **Split panes** (horizontal and vertical)
- **Ghostty config compatibility** — reads `~/.config/ghostty/config` for themes, fonts, colors

## Notification System
- **Terminal sequence detection** (OSC 9/99/777)
- **CLI command**: `cmux notify` for agent integration
- **Visual indicators**: Blue rings on panes, highlighted tabs when agents need attention
- **Notification panel** with jump-to-latest-unread functionality (Cmd+Shift+U)

## In-App Browser
- **Scriptable API** (ported from agent-browser)
- Browser capabilities: accessibility tree snapshots, element references, clicking, form filling, JavaScript evaluation
- Can split browser alongside terminal for agent interaction with dev servers

## Scriptability & Automation
- **CLI interface** for workspaces, tabs, panes, keystrokes
- **Socket API** for programmatic control
- **Agent hooks** — integrates with Claude Code, OpenCode, etc.

## Session Management
- Window/workspace/pane layout restoration
- Working directory persistence
- Terminal scrollback recovery (best effort)
- Browser URL and history retention
- ⚠️ Does NOT restore live process state (tmux, vim, active sessions)

## Configuration
- Reads existing Ghostty configuration files
- Customizable keyboard shortcuts via Settings

## Workspace Organization
- Multiple workspaces with numbering (1-8, plus last)
- Tab management and renaming

"Each workspace is just a folder on your machine," allowing you to open workspaces in VS Code, Zed, terminals, or preferred development tools.

## Tech Stack

Built with Electron, React, TailwindCSS, Bun, Turborepo, Vite, Biome, Drizzle ORM, Neon, and tRPC.

## Notable Limitation

**Platform Support**: Currently tested and built only for macOS; Windows and Linux support status remains unofficial.

---

# Research Notes: awesome-zellij Plugins, Superset.sh, and cmux vs. dellij

---

## Part 1: awesome-zellij — All Plugins and Tools

### Navigation
- **harpoon** — Quickly navigate panes; clone of neovim's harpoon
- **room** — Fuzzy search and switch tabs
- **vim-zellij-navigator** — Seamless pane navigation integrated with Vim/Neovim
- **zellij-jump-list** — Pane-to-pane motion history, similar to Vim/Emacs jump lists
- **zellij-nvim-nav-plugin** — Another Neovim/Vim seamless navigation plugin
- **zellij-pane-picker** — Switch, star, and jump to panes with customizable shortcuts
- **zjpane** — Navigate between panes easily

### Session Management
- **zbuffers** — Minimal tab/session switcher inspired by Emacs vertico-buffers
- **zellij-attention** — Adds notification icons to tab names when panes need attention; designed for Claude Code
- **zellij-autolock** — Auto-locks Zellij based on focused pane command; pairs with zellij.vim for seamless Vim navigation
- **zellij-bookmarks** — Manage command bookmarks and quickly insert them into the terminal
- **zellij-cb** — Customizable compact status bar
- **zellij-choose-tree** — Switch between sessions; inspired by tmux choose-tree
- **zellij-favs** — Save favorite sessions, flush the rest
- **zellij-sessionizer** — Create sessions from folder names
- **zellij-switch** — CLI-driven session switching using `zellij pipe`
- **zellij-worktree** — Zellij plugin for managing git worktrees
- **zsm** — Zoxide-integrated session switcher with default layout support

### Status Bar
- **zellaude** — Status bar plugin showing Claude Code activity indicators on tabs
- **zellij-datetime** — Adds date/time pane
- **zellij-load** — Shows CPU, memory, GPU usage (similar to tmux cpu-usage)
- **zellij-what-time** — Shows host system date/time in status bar
- **zj-status-bar** — Opinionated fork of compact-bar
- **zjstatus** — Configurable, themeable status bar plugin
- **zjstatus-hints** — Mode-aware keybinding hints overlay for zjstatus

### UI & Modes
- **zellij-forgot** — Present and access keybinds on demand
- **zellij-getmode** — Utility to read current Zellij input mode
- **zellij-layoutswitch** — Switch between layouts without shell bloat
- **zellij-newtab-plus** — Create named tabs and navigate with zoxide in one keybind
- **zellij-tab-bar-indexed** — Numeric tab indices for quick keyboard navigation
- **zellij-vertical-tabs** — Display tabs as vertical rows in sidebar
- **zellij-workspace** — Apply layouts to current session
- **zjswitcher** — Auto-switch between normal and locked mode

### Search
- **grab** — Fuzzy finder for Rust devs (files, structs, enums, functions)
- **monocle** — Fuzzy file name and content search

### Utilities
- **ghost** — Spawn floating command terminal pane (interactive)
- **multitask** — Mini-CI as a Zellij plugin; run parallel tasks with pass/fail tracking
- **zellij-notepad** — Floating notepad pane with timestamped notes
- **zellij-playbooks** — Browse, select, and execute commands from playbook YAML/TOML files
- **zellij-qr-share** — Show web tokens as QR codes for mobile authentication
- **zellij-send-keys** — Send text/commands to specific panes (like tmux send-keys)
- **zj-quit** — Friendly quit plugin

### External Tools / Integrations
- **gitpod.zellij** — Zellij plugin for Gitpod with `.gitpod.yml` tasks integration
- **jbz (Just Bacon Zellij)** — Display `just` commands wrapped in bacon
- **zellijira** — Manage sessions around Jira issues
- **zellij-theme-configurator** — Web GUI for creating and exporting Zellij themes
- **zellij-vscode-toolkit** — VS Code extension with IntelliSense/validation for Zellij KDL config files
- **zj-docker** — Display Docker containers and perform basic operations
- **zj-git-branch** — Manage git branches
- **fzf-zellij** — Shell script to start fzf in a Zellij floating pane
- **opencode-zellij-namer** — AI-powered dynamic session naming for OpenCode; renames sessions based on project context
- **theylix** — Zellij + Helix + Yazi + Lazygit + LazySQL + Slumber as a zen IDE
- **yazelix** — Zellij + Yazi + Nushell; adds file tree to Helix with Helix-friendly keybindings
- **zeco** — Share Zellij sessions over the internet securely
- **zellij-sessionizer** (victor-falcon) — Fuzzy-finder project switcher inspired by ThePrimeagen/tmux-sessionizer
- **zellix** — Nushell wrapper over Helix using Zellij as a plugin system
- **zide** — Zellij layouts + bash scripts for IDE-like file picker/editor workflow
- **zrw** — Run commands in Zellij panes, wait for completion, propagate exit codes

---

## Part 2: Superset.sh — Feature Notes

**What it is:** A macOS desktop application (Electron/React) that manages multiple parallel AI coding agent workspaces, each isolated in a git worktree.

### Core Features
- **Git worktree isolation** — each workspace = one git branch in its own worktree directory; own working directory, terminal, and port namespace
- **Parallel agent execution** — run dozens of agents simultaneously (Claude Code, OpenCode, OpenAI Codex, and any CLI-based agent)
- **Workspace creation flows** — create from new branch, existing branch, or PR; bulk-import existing worktrees
- **Built-in multi-tab terminal** — multiple terminal tabs per workspace; session persistence across app restarts (running processes continue, scrollback preserved)
- **Built-in diff viewer** — side-by-side or inline diff view of agent changes without leaving the app
- **Built-in lightweight code editor** — review and edit agent changes inline
- **PR integration** — PR-based workspaces show review status, CI check status, GitHub links, deployment preview buttons
- **Commit status indicators** — ↑N (unpushed commits) and ↓N (behind remote) shown per workspace
- **IDE deep-linking** — open any workspace in VS Code, Cursor, Xcode, JetBrains IDEs, or terminal with one click
- **Port forwarding/detection** — workspace-scoped port detection and controls
- **MCP server support** — Model Context Protocol server integration
- **Real-time agent monitoring** — centralized view of all agent status; agents notify when tasks complete or encounter issues
- **Environment variable injection** — `SUPERSET_ROOT_PATH` and `SUPERSET_WORKSPACE_NAME` available in each terminal
- **`.superset/config.json`** — per-project configuration file for workspace settings
- **Self-hostable / open source** — Elastic License 2.0; source inspection available
- **Free tier** — personal API keys, no proxying required

### Unique Differentiators
- Full native GUI application with integrated diff/editor/browser
- PR-first workflow: create workspace from a PR URL/number, see CI status and deployment previews inline
- One-click IDE handoff (VS Code, Cursor, JetBrains, Xcode)
- Commit staging and message authoring within the app
- MCP server built in

---

## Part 3: cmux — Feature Notes

**What it is:** A native macOS terminal application (Swift/AppKit, libghostty rendering) designed specifically for running and orchestrating multiple AI coding agents.

### Core Features
- **Workspaces** — create, switch (⌘1-9), rename multiple independent workspaces
- **Split panes** — horizontal and vertical splits (⌘D, ⌘⇧D)
- **Vertical tab sidebar** — shows per-workspace: git branch, PR status/number, working directory, listening ports, latest notification text
- **Notification system** — visual blue rings on panes + highlighted sidebar tabs when agents need attention; captures OSC 9/99/777 terminal sequences; centralized notification panel with jump-to-latest (⌘⇧U)
- **In-app browser** — full scriptable browser split alongside terminal; accessibility tree snapshots, element references (short IDs like `e10`), clicking, form filling, JavaScript evaluation; developer tools support
- **CLI interface** (`cmux` binary) — full programmatic control via Unix domain socket (`/tmp/cmux.sock`) for near-instantaneous IPC
- **Session persistence** — restores window layout, working directories, scrollback, browser history on relaunch (does not restore live process state)
- **Ghostty config compatibility** — reads `~/.config/ghostty/config` for themes, fonts, colors
- **Claude Code hooks** — automatic triggering of scripts on session/task lifecycle events
- **`set-status`** — update sidebar status text with SF Symbol icons per workspace
- **`set-progress`** — show progress bars in sidebar per workspace
- **`list-notifications`** — enumerate pending agent notifications
- **`read-screen`** — read terminal pane content programmatically
- **`trigger-flash`** — visual flash alert on specific panes
- **`send` / `send-key`** — type text or simulate keypresses in any pane
- **`new-pane --type browser --url`** — open browser pane programmatically
- **Browser subcommands** — `navigate`, `wait --load-state`, `snapshot --interactive`, `type`, `click`, `eval`
- **`notify --title --body`** — send macOS system notifications from agent scripts

### Unique Differentiators
- **Sub-agent orchestration** — a primary agent can programmatically spawn sub-agents in their own panes, send them instructions via `send`/`send-key`, poll their status via `read-screen` and `set-status`, and collect results — all within one cmux workspace
- **In-app browser automation with DOM references** — browser snapshots return short stable element IDs enabling reliable automated web interaction (no separate Playwright/Puppeteer needed)
- **Unix socket IPC** — near-zero-latency programmatic control from inside any agent
- **SF Symbols sidebar icons** — rich ambient status display per workspace without terminal output
- **Progress bars** — `set-progress` gives per-workspace visual progress indication in sidebar
- **MCP server** — `cmux-agent-mcp` and `cmuxlayer` MCP servers available for agent tool use
- Native Swift/AppKit (no Electron), GPU-accelerated via libghostty

---

## Part 4: Gap Analysis — Features NOT in dellij

Based on reading `/home/diogenes/Projects/dellij/crates/dellij/src/main.rs` and the full feature inventories above, here are features present in superset and/or cmux that are absent from dellij:

### UI / Visualization
- **No GUI** — dellij is pure CLI/TUI; superset has a full Electron GUI with diff viewer, inline editor, PR status dashboard; cmux has a native macOS GUI with sidebar ambient display
- **No sidebar status display** — cmux's sidebar shows git branch, PR number, working directory, ports, and notification text as ambient information per workspace; dellij has a Zellij status plugin but it is far less rich
- **No progress bars** — cmux's `set-progress` gives per-workspace visual progress indication; dellij has no equivalent
- **No pane flash/visual alerts** — cmux's `trigger-flash` provides a visual attention mechanism; dellij has no visual alerting

### Notifications
- **No push notification system** — neither superset nor cmux require agents to explicitly poll; cmux captures OSC terminal sequences automatically and fires macOS system notifications; dellij has no notification delivery mechanism
- **No notification queue/panel** — cmux maintains a centralized list of pending notifications (`list-notifications`) with jump-to-latest; dellij has no equivalent
- **No Claude Code hook integration** — cmux auto-fires scripts on Claude Code session/task lifecycle events without agent cooperation; dellij's hooks (`workspace_setup`, `workspace_teardown`) are only triggered by explicit dellij CLI calls

### Git / PR Integration
- **No PR-based workspace creation** — superset lets you create a workspace from a PR URL, number, or title and tracks CI status + review state; dellij only creates new branches from a base branch
- **No commit status indicators** — superset shows ahead/behind counts per workspace; dellij does not
- **No CI/PR status tracking** — superset shows review status, CI check results, deployment preview links; dellij tracks only its own `working/waiting/blocked/review/done/error` states, not external CI data
- **No import of existing worktrees** — superset has bulk-import for existing git worktrees; dellij requires creating new ones through its own flow
- **No branch deletion on close tied to PR merge detection** — superset can detect post-merge and prompt for workspace deletion; dellij's `close` command removes the worktree but has no merge-awareness

### Built-in Code Review
- **No diff viewer** — superset has a built-in side-by-side/inline diff viewer; dellij has none
- **No built-in editor** — superset has a lightweight editor for reviewing/editing agent changes; dellij has none
- **No commit staging UI** — superset can stage changes and compose commit messages; dellij has no git staging capability

### Browser / Web
- **No in-app browser** — cmux has a full scriptable browser pane; dellij has no browser integration
- **No browser automation API** — cmux provides DOM snapshots, element references, click/type/eval; dellij has no equivalent
- **No web preview integration** — superset shows deployment preview buttons tied to PRs; dellij has no preview concept

### Agent Orchestration
- **No sub-agent spawning** — cmux allows a primary agent to programmatically create panes, send instructions to sub-agents, and collect results; dellij has no mechanism for agent-to-agent orchestration
- **No `read-screen`** — cmux allows agents to read terminal pane content programmatically; dellij has no equivalent
- **No `send`/`send-key` to arbitrary panes** — cmux allows sending text/keystrokes to any pane from scripts; dellij has no pane-level IPC
- **No Unix socket / IPC API** — cmux exposes a socket API for near-zero-latency control; dellij is invoked only via subprocess (Zellij CLI calls)
- **No MCP server** — cmux and superset both have MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration; dellij has none

### Session / Workspace Lifecycle
- **No session persistence across restarts** — superset and cmux both restore terminal state/scrollback/layout; dellij creates Zellij sessions but does not manage restoration
- **No workspace creation from existing branches** — dellij always creates a new branch; superset can open existing branches
- **No "waiting for input" detection** — cmux and superset detect when agents are blocked/awaiting input automatically; dellij relies on agents manually calling `dellij status <slug> waiting`
- **No port forwarding** — superset has workspace-scoped port forwarding controls; dellij tracks port metadata but performs no actual forwarding

### IDE Integration
- **No IDE deep-linking** — superset can open any workspace in VS Code, Cursor, Xcode, JetBrains IDEs with one click; dellij has no IDE handoff
- **`SUPERSET_ROOT_PATH` / `SUPERSET_WORKSPACE_NAME` env vars** — superset injects these automatically; dellij injects `DELLIJ_*` vars only inside hook scripts, not in the agent's terminal environment by default

### Configuration & Settings
- **No per-project UI config** — superset uses `.superset/config.json`; dellij uses `.dellij/dellij.json` (similar, but no GUI)
- **No hot-reload of config** — cmux supports `⌘⇧,` hot reload; dellij reads config fresh on each invocation (stateless CLI)
- **No theming/font controls** — cmux inherits Ghostty config; dellij delegates all rendering to Zellij's own config

### Ecosystem / Discovery
- **No zoxide integration** — `zsm` (awesome-zellij) and other tools use zoxide for project discovery; dellij uses only git worktrees
- **No fuzzy project/session finder** — tools like `zellij-sessionizer`, `zsm`, `zellij-choose-tree` provide fuzzy session switching; dellij has `list` but no interactive picker
- **No Jira/issue tracker integration** — zellijira maps sessions to Jira issues; dellij has no issue tracker linkage
- **No Docker integration** — `zj-docker` provides Docker container management; dellij has no container awareness
- **No multi-tab terminal per workspace** — superset supports multiple terminal tabs within a single workspace; dellij creates one Zellij tab per workspace with a fixed 3-pane layout

---

### Session Management
