- **zellij-favs** — Save favorite sessions, flush the rest
- **zellij-sessionizer** — Create sessions from folder names
- **zellij-switch** — CLI-driven session switching using `zellij pipe`
- **zellij-worktree** — Zellij plugin for managing git worktrees
- **zsm** — Zoxide-integrated session switcher with default layout support

### Status Bar
- **zellaude** — Status bar plugin showing Claude Code activity indicators on tabs
- **zellij-datetime** — Adds date/time pane
- **zellij-load** — Shows CPU, memory, GPU usage (similar to tmux cpu-usage)
- **zellij-what-time** — Shows host system date/time in status bar
- **zj-status-bar** — Opinionated fork of compact-bar
- **zjstatus** — Configurable, themeable status bar plugin
- **zjstatus-hints** — Mode-aware keybinding hints overlay for zjstatus

### UI & Modes
- **zellij-forgot** — Present and access keybinds on demand
- **zellij-getmode** — Utility to read current Zellij input mode
- **zellij-layoutswitch** — Switch between layouts without shell bloat
- **zellij-newtab-plus** — Create named tabs and navigate with zoxide in one keybind
- **zellij-tab-bar-indexed** — Numeric tab indices for quick keyboard navigation
- **zellij-vertical-tabs** — Display tabs as vertical rows in sidebar
- **zellij-workspace** — Apply layouts to current session
- **zjswitcher** — Auto-switch between normal and locked mode

### Search
- **grab** — Fuzzy finder for Rust devs (files, structs, enums, functions)
- **monocle** — Fuzzy file name and content search

### Utilities
- **ghost** — Spawn floating command terminal pane (interactive)
- **multitask** — Mini-CI as a Zellij plugin; run parallel tasks with pass/fail tracking
- **zellij-notepad** — Floating notepad pane with timestamped notes
- **zellij-playbooks** — Browse, select, and execute commands from playbook YAML/TOML files
- **zellij-qr-share** — Show web tokens as QR codes for mobile authentication
- **zellij-send-keys** — Send text/commands to specific panes (like tmux send-keys)
- **zj-quit** — Friendly quit plugin

### External Tools / Integrations
- **gitpod.zellij** — Zellij plugin for Gitpod with `.gitpod.yml` tasks integration
- **jbz (Just Bacon Zellij)** — Display `just` commands wrapped in bacon
- **zellijira** — Manage sessions around Jira issues
- **zellij-theme-configurator** — Web GUI for creating and exporting Zellij themes
- **zellij-vscode-toolkit** — VS Code extension with IntelliSense/validation for Zellij KDL config files
- **zj-docker** — Display Docker containers and perform basic operations
- **zj-git-branch** — Manage git branches
- **fzf-zellij** — Shell script to start fzf in a Zellij floating pane
- **opencode-zellij-namer** — AI-powered dynamic session naming for OpenCode; renames sessions based on project context
- **theylix** — Zellij + Helix + Yazi + Lazygit + LazySQL + Slumber as a zen IDE
- **yazelix** — Zellij + Yazi + Nushell; adds file tree to Helix with Helix-friendly keybindings
- **zeco** — Share Zellij sessions over the internet securely
- **zellij-sessionizer** (victor-falcon) — Fuzzy-finder project switcher inspired by ThePrimeagen/tmux-sessionizer
- **zellix** — Nushell wrapper over Helix using Zellij as a plugin system
- **zide** — Zellij layouts + bash scripts for IDE-like file picker/editor workflow
- **zrw** — Run commands in Zellij panes, wait for completion, propagate exit codes

---

## Part 2: Superset.sh — Feature Notes

**What it is:** A macOS desktop application (Electron/React) that manages multiple parallel AI coding agent workspaces, each isolated in a git worktree.

### Core Features
- **Git worktree isolation** — each workspace = one git branch in its own worktree directory; own working directory, terminal, and port namespace
- **Parallel agent execution** — run dozens of agents simultaneously (Claude Code, OpenCode, OpenAI Codex, and any CLI-based agent)
- **Workspace creation flows** — create from new branch, existing branch, or PR; bulk-import existing worktrees
- **Built-in multi-tab terminal** — multiple terminal tabs per workspace; session persistence across app restarts (running processes continue, scrollback preserved)
- **Built-in diff viewer** — side-by-side or inline diff view of agent changes without leaving the app
- **Built-in lightweight code editor** — review and edit agent changes inline
- **PR integration** — PR-based workspaces show review status, CI check status, GitHub links, deployment preview buttons
- **Commit status indicators** — ↑N (unpushed commits) and ↓N (behind remote) shown per workspace
- **IDE deep-linking** — open any workspace in VS Code, Cursor, Xcode, JetBrains IDEs, or terminal with one click
- **Port forwarding/detection** — workspace-scoped port detection and controls
- **MCP server support** — Model Context Protocol server integration
- **Real-time agent monitoring** — centralized view of all agent status; agents notify when tasks complete or encounter issues
- **Environment variable injection** — `SUPERSET_ROOT_PATH` and `SUPERSET_WORKSPACE_NAME` available in each terminal
- **`.superset/config.json`** — per-project configuration file for workspace settings
- **Self-hostable / open source** — Elastic License 2.0; source inspection available
- **Free tier** — personal API keys, no proxying required

### Unique Differentiators
- Full native GUI application with integrated diff/editor/browser
- PR-first workflow: create workspace from a PR URL/number, see CI status and deployment previews inline
- One-click IDE handoff (VS Code, Cursor, JetBrains, Xcode)
- Commit staging and message authoring within the app
- MCP server built in

---

## Part 3: cmux — Feature Notes

**What it is:** A native macOS terminal application (Swift/AppKit, libghostty rendering) designed specifically for running and orchestrating multiple AI coding agents.

### Core Features
- **Workspaces** — create, switch (⌘1-9), rename multiple independent workspaces
- **Split panes** — horizontal and vertical splits (⌘D, ⌘⇧D)
- **Vertical tab sidebar** — shows per-workspace: git branch, PR status/number, working directory, listening ports, latest notification text
- **Notification system** — visual blue rings on panes + highlighted sidebar tabs when agents need attention; captures OSC 9/99/777 terminal sequences; centralized notification panel with jump-to-latest (⌘⇧U)
- **In-app browser** — full scriptable browser split alongside terminal; accessibility tree snapshots, element references (short IDs like `e10`), clicking, form filling, JavaScript evaluation; developer tools support
- **CLI interface** (`cmux` binary) — full programmatic control via Unix domain socket (`/tmp/cmux.sock`) for near-instantaneous IPC
- **Session persistence** — restores window layout, working directories, scrollback, browser history on relaunch (does not restore live process state)
- **Ghostty config compatibility** — reads `~/.config/ghostty/config` for themes, fonts, colors
- **Claude Code hooks** — automatic triggering of scripts on session/task lifecycle events
- **`set-status`** — update sidebar status text with SF Symbol icons per workspace
- **`set-progress`** — show progress bars in sidebar per workspace
- **`list-notifications`** — enumerate pending agent notifications
- **`read-screen`** — read terminal pane content programmatically
- **`trigger-flash`** — visual flash alert on specific panes
- **`send` / `send-key`** — type text or simulate keypresses in any pane
- **`new-pane --type browser --url`** — open browser pane programmatically
- **Browser subcommands** — `navigate`, `wait --load-state`, `snapshot --interactive`, `type`, `click`, `eval`
- **`notify --title --body`** — send macOS system notifications from agent scripts

### Unique Differentiators
- **Sub-agent orchestration** — a primary agent can programmatically spawn sub-agents in their own panes, send them instructions via `send`/`send-key`, poll their status via `read-screen` and `set-status`, and collect results — all within one cmux workspace
- **In-app browser automation with DOM references** — browser snapshots return short stable element IDs enabling reliable automated web interaction (no separate Playwright/Puppeteer needed)
- **Unix socket IPC** — near-zero-latency programmatic control from inside any agent
- **SF Symbols sidebar icons** — rich ambient status display per workspace without terminal output
- **Progress bars** — `set-progress` gives per-workspace visual progress indication in sidebar
- **MCP server** — `cmux-agent-mcp` and `cmuxlayer` MCP servers available for agent tool use
- Native Swift/AppKit (no Electron), GPU-accelerated via libghostty

---

## Part 4: Gap Analysis — Features NOT in dellij

Based on reading `/home/diogenes/Projects/dellij/crates/dellij/src/main.rs` and the full feature inventories above, here are features present in superset and/or cmux that are absent from dellij:

### UI / Visualization
- **No GUI** — dellij is pure CLI/TUI; superset has a full Electron GUI with diff viewer, inline editor, PR status dashboard; cmux has a native macOS GUI with sidebar ambient display
- **No sidebar status display** — cmux's sidebar shows git branch, PR number, working directory, ports, and notification text as ambient information per workspace; dellij has a Zellij status plugin but it is far less rich
- **No progress bars** — cmux's `set-progress` gives per-workspace visual progress indication; dellij has no equivalent
- **No pane flash/visual alerts** — cmux's `trigger-flash` provides a visual attention mechanism; dellij has no visual alerting

### Notifications
- **No push notification system** — neither superset nor cmux require agents to explicitly poll; cmux captures OSC terminal sequences automatically and fires macOS system notifications; dellij has no notification delivery mechanism
- **No notification queue/panel** — cmux maintains a centralized list of pending notifications (`list-notifications`) with jump-to-latest; dellij has no equivalent
- **No Claude Code hook integration** — cmux auto-fires scripts on Claude Code session/task lifecycle events without agent cooperation; dellij's hooks (`workspace_setup`, `workspace_teardown`) are only triggered by explicit dellij CLI calls

### Git / PR Integration
- **No PR-based workspace creation** — superset lets you create a workspace from a PR URL, number, or title and tracks CI status + review state; dellij only creates new branches from a base branch
- **No commit status indicators** — superset shows ahead/behind counts per workspace; dellij does not
- **No CI/PR status tracking** — superset shows review status, CI check results, deployment preview links; dellij tracks only its own `working/waiting/blocked/review/done/error` states, not external CI data
- **No import of existing worktrees** — superset has bulk-import for existing git worktrees; dellij requires creating new ones through its own flow
- **No branch deletion on close tied to PR merge detection** — superset can detect post-merge and prompt for workspace deletion; dellij's `close` command removes the worktree but has no merge-awareness

### Built-in Code Review
- **No diff viewer** — superset has a built-in side-by-side/inline diff viewer; dellij has none
- **No built-in editor** — superset has a lightweight editor for reviewing/editing agent changes; dellij has none
- **No commit staging UI** — superset can stage changes and compose commit messages; dellij has no git staging capability

### Browser / Web
- **No in-app browser** — cmux has a full scriptable browser pane; dellij has no browser integration
- **No browser automation API** — cmux provides DOM snapshots, element references, click/type/eval; dellij has no equivalent
- **No web preview integration** — superset shows deployment preview buttons tied to PRs; dellij has no preview concept

### Agent Orchestration
- **No sub-agent spawning** — cmux allows a primary agent to programmatically create panes, send instructions to sub-agents, and collect results; dellij has no mechanism for agent-to-agent orchestration
- **No `read-screen`** — cmux allows agents to read terminal pane content programmatically; dellij has no equivalent
- **No `send`/`send-key` to arbitrary panes** — cmux allows sending text/keystrokes to any pane from scripts; dellij has no pane-level IPC
- **No Unix socket / IPC API** — cmux exposes a socket API for near-zero-latency control; dellij is invoked only via subprocess (Zellij CLI calls)
- **No MCP server** — cmux and superset both have MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration; dellij has none

### Session / Workspace Lifecycle
- **No session persistence across restarts** — superset and cmux both restore terminal state/scrollback/layout; dellij creates Zellij sessions but does not manage restoration
- **No workspace creation from existing branches** — dellij always creates a new branch; superset can open existing branches
- **No "waiting for input" detection** — cmux and superset detect when agents are blocked/awaiting input automatically; dellij relies on agents manually calling `dellij status <slug> waiting`
- **No port forwarding** — superset has workspace-scoped port forwarding controls; dellij tracks port metadata but performs no actual forwarding

### IDE Integration
- **No IDE deep-linking** — superset can open any workspace in VS Code, Cursor, Xcode, JetBrains IDEs with one click; dellij has no IDE handoff
- **`SUPERSET_ROOT_PATH` / `SUPERSET_WORKSPACE_NAME` env vars** — superset injects these automatically; dellij injects `DELLIJ_*` vars only inside hook scripts, not in the agent's terminal environment by default

### Configuration & Settings
- **No per-project UI config** — superset uses `.superset/config.json`; dellij uses `.dellij/dellij.json` (similar, but no GUI)
- **No hot-reload of config** — cmux supports `⌘⇧,` hot reload; dellij reads config fresh on each invocation (stateless CLI)
- **No theming/font controls** — cmux inherits Ghostty config; dellij delegates all rendering to Zellij's own config

### Ecosystem / Discovery
- **No zoxide integration** — `zsm` (awesome-zellij) and other tools use zoxide for project discovery; dellij uses only git worktrees
- **No fuzzy project/session finder** — tools like `zellij-sessionizer`, `zsm`, `zellij-choose-tree` provide fuzzy session switching; dellij has `list` but no interactive picker
- **No Jira/issue tracker integration** — zellijira maps sessions to Jira issues; dellij has no issue tracker linkage
- **No Docker integration** — `zj-docker` provides Docker container management; dellij has no container awareness
- **No multi-tab terminal per workspace** — superset supports multiple terminal tabs within a single workspace; dellij creates one Zellij tab per workspace with a fixed 3-pane layout

---

Sources:
- [awesome-zellij (via gh CLI)](https://github.com/zellij-org/awesome-zellij)
- [Superset - Run 10+ parallel coding agents on your machine](https://superset.sh/)
- [Superset Workspaces Docs](https://docs.superset.sh/workspaces)
- [Superset Terminal Integration Docs](https://docs.superset.sh/terminal-integration)
- [Superset Monorepo/CLI DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/superset-sh/superset/4.1-cli-commands)
- [Superset overview - Medevel](https://medevel.com/superset/)
- [cmux — The terminal built for multitasking](https://cmux.com/)
- [GitHub - manaflow-ai/cmux](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux)

content.content:
# cmux README

**cmux** is a native macOS terminal application built with Swift and AppKit, designed specifically for AI coding agents. It extends Ghostty with vertical tabs and a notification system.

## Core Features

The application provides "notification rings" where "panes get a blue ring and tabs light up when coding agents need your attention." A notification panel allows users to view all pending alerts in one place.

An integrated browser component offers a "scriptable API ported from agent-browser" enabling agents to interact with web interfaces. The sidebar displays "git branch, linked PR status/number, working directory, listening ports, and latest notification text."

## Technical Specifications

cmux is "built with Swift and AppKit, not Electron" and uses libghostty for rendering. It reads existing Ghostty configuration files for themes and fonts, maintaining compatibility with users' established setups.

## Installation & Access

The application is available via DMG download or Homebrew installation. Auto-updates are handled through Sparkle, requiring only a single initial download.

## Philosophy

The developers emphasize that "cmux is not prescriptive about how developers hold their tools" and functions as "a primitive, not a solution," providing composable components rather than enforcing specific workflows.

## Licensing

cmux is open source under AGPL-3.0-or-later, though commercial licenses are available for organizations unable to comply with AGPL requirements.

# cmux Repository Analysis

## 1. Full README Content Summary

**cmux** is a native macOS terminal application built with Swift/AppKit that combines Ghostty terminal emulation with vertical tabs, notifications, and an in-app browser. Key features include:

- **Notification system**: Visual rings around panes and tab highlighting when agents need attention
- **In-app browser**: Scriptable API for agent interaction with web applications
- **Vertical + horizontal tabs**: Sidebar displays git branches, PR status, working directories, ports, and notifications
- **Native performance**: GPU-accelerated rendering via libghostty
- **Ghostty compatible**: Reads existing `~/.config/ghostty/config` for themes and fonts

Installation options: DMG download or Homebrew (`brew tap manaflow-ai/cmux && brew install --cask cmux`)

## 2. File Tree Structure

**Directories**: `.claude/commands`, `.github`, `AppIcon.icon`, `Assets.xcassets`, `CLI`, `GhosttyTabs.xcodeproj`, `Resources`, `Sources`, `cmuxTests`, `cmuxUITests`, `daemon/remote`, `design`, `docs`, `ghostty` (submodule), `homebrew-cmux` (submodule), `node_modules`, `scripts`, `skills`, `tests`, `tests_v2`, `vendor`, `web`

**Key Files**: `README.md` (English + 18 translations), `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `LICENSE`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `PROJECTS.md`, `Package.swift`, `cmux.entitlements`, `ghostty.h`

## 3. CLI Documentation

The README references `cmux notify` command for hooking agent notifications into the system. Full command reference appears truncated in the provided content, but keyboard shortcuts indicate comprehensive CLI functionality for:

- Creating workspaces/surfaces
- Splitting panes
- Sending keystrokes
- Browser automation

## 4. Unix Socket API Details

The documentation mentions a "socket API" for scriptable control but specific socket endpoint details, message formats, or protocol specifications are not included in the provided excerpt.

## 5. Hook System Documentation

The system supports "terminal sequences (OSC 9/99/777)" and mentions wiring hooks into "Claude Code, OpenCode, etc." agent systems. Specific hook interface documentation is not detailed in the visible content.

## 6. MCP Server Details

No MCP (Model Context Protocol) server information appears in the provided excerpt.

---

**License**: AGPL-3.0-or-later with commercial licensing available; contact `founders@manaflow.com`

# Claude Code Development Guide for cmux

This document outlines development workflows for the cmux terminal multiplexer project.

## Key Development Practices

**Build and Launch Protocol**: Always use `./scripts/reload.sh --tag <name>` when testing code changes. Never run bare `xcodebuild` or launch an untagged `cmux DEV.app`, as this causes socket conflicts between concurrent agent instances.

**Reporting Results**: When sharing tagged build results in chat, use file:// URLs pointing to the actual DerivedData path, not `/tmp` paths. The format should be clickable and derived-data specific.

**Debug Logging**: All debug events route to a unified log file. Untagged debug builds write to `/tmp/cmux-debug.log`, while tagged builds use `/tmp/cmux-debug-<tag>.log`. The `reload.sh` script updates `/tmp/cmux-last-debug-log-path` with the current log location.

## Critical Pitfalls

**Performance Sensitivity**: Several code paths directly impact typing latency:
- `WindowTerminalHostView.hitTest()` handles all events including keyboard; pointer-event routing must remain gated
- `TabItemView` uses equatable conformance to skip re-evaluation during typing; do not add new environment objects without updating equality logic
- `TerminalSurface.forceRefresh()` executes per keystroke; avoid allocations or I/O here

**Submodule Safety**: When modifying submodules (ghostty, bonsplit), commit changes to the submodule's remote main branch before updating the parent repo pointer. Commits on detached heads get orphaned.

**Localization**: All user-facing strings must use `String(localized: "key.name", defaultValue: "text")` with entries in `Resources/Localizable.xcstrings`.

## Testing Requirements

Never run tests locally. E2E and UI tests use GitHub Actions; unit tests should prefer CI as well. For socket tests requiring a running instance, use a tagged build's socket path with the `CMUX_SOCKET` environment variable.

content.content:
# Summary of Claude Code CLI

This is a comprehensive Swift implementation of a CLI tool for managing the cmux terminal application. Here are the key components:

## Core Architecture

**Socket Communication**: The `SocketClient` class handles Unix domain socket connections with:
- Connection verification (ownership checks to prevent fake-socket attacks)
- V1 and V2 protocol support
- Configurable timeouts and multiline response handling
- File system watching for delayed socket availability

**Telemetry**: The `CLISocketSentryTelemetry` class integrates error reporting via Sentry, capturing:
- Command execution context
- Socket diagnostics (permissions, file type, ownership)
- Automatic detection of configuration issues

## Command Categories

The CLI supports numerous command families:

1. **Window Management**: create, focus, close, list windows
2. **Workspace Operations**: create, rename, select, close workspaces
3. **Pane/Surface Control**: split, focus, reorder surfaces and panes
4. **Text Operations**: send text/keys to surfaces, read screen content
5. **Notifications**: create and manage notifications
6. **Sidebar Metadata**: status, progress, and logging functionality
7. **Browser Integration**: navigate, reload, get URLs from web views
8. **Markdown Support**: open markdown files in dedicated surfaces
9. **SSH Sessions**: remote connection management
10. **Tmux Compatibility**: bridge commands for tmux-like workflows

## Key Features

- **Smart Socket Resolution**: Discovers available sockets via `CLISocketPathResolver`, preferring active connections
- **Session Tracking**: `ClaudeHookSessionStore` maintains persistent session state with file-based locking
- **Flexible ID Handling**: Supports refs, UUIDs, or both formats via `CLIIDFormat`
- **Password Management**: Retrieves credentials from explicit args, environment, files, or system keychain
- **Path Handling**: Expands tildes, resolves relative paths, sanitizes filenames

## Authentication & Security

- Socket ownership validation
- Keychain-based password storage with scoped retrieval
- Environment variable precedence for configuration
- File-based locking for concurrent access safety

The implementation demonstrates production-grade CLI design with comprehensive error handling, telemetry integration, and flexible configuration options.

# Remote SSH Living Spec

Last updated: March 12, 2026
Tracking issue: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux/issues/151
Primary PR: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux/pull/1296
CLI relay PR: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux/pull/374

This document is the working source of truth for:
1. what is implemented now
2. what is intentionally temporary
3. what must be built next

## 1. Document Type

This is a **living implementation spec** (also called an **execution spec**): a spec-level document with status tracking (`DONE`, `IN PROGRESS`, `TODO`) and acceptance tests.

## 2. Objective

`cmux ssh` should provide:
1. durable remote terminals with reconnect/reuse
2. browser traffic that egresses from the remote host via proxying
3. tmux-style PTY resize semantics (`smallest screen wins`)

## 3. Current State (Implemented)

### 3.1 Remote Workspace + Reconnect UX
- `DONE` `cmux ssh` creates remote-tagged workspaces and does not require `--name`.
- `DONE` scoped shell niceties are applied only for `cmux ssh` launches.
- `DONE` context menu actions exist for remote workspaces (`Reconnect Workspace(s)`, `Disconnect Workspace(s)`).
- `DONE` socket API includes `workspace.remote.reconnect`.

### 3.2 Bootstrap + Daemon
- `DONE` local app probes remote platform, verifies a release-pinned `cmuxd-remote` artifact by embedded manifest SHA-256, uploads it when missing, and runs `serve --stdio`.
- `DONE` daemon `hello` handshake is enforced.
- `DONE` daemon now exposes proxy stream RPC (`proxy.open`, `proxy.close`, `proxy.write`, `proxy.stream.subscribe`) plus pushed `proxy.stream.*` events.
- `DONE` local proxy broker now tunnels SOCKS5/CONNECT traffic over daemon stream RPC instead of `ssh -D`.
- `DONE` daemon now exposes session resize-coordinator RPC (`session.open`, `session.attach`, `session.resize`, `session.detach`, `session.status`, `session.close`).
- `DONE` transport-level proxy failures now escalate from broker retry to full daemon re-bootstrap/reconnect in the session controller.
- `DONE` SOCKS handshake parsing now preserves pipelined post-connect payload bytes instead of dropping request-prefix bytes.
- `DONE` `workspace.remote.configure.local_proxy_port` exists as an internal deterministic test hook for bind-conflict regression coverage.
- `DONE` bootstrap/probe failures surface actionable details.
- `DONE` bootstrap installs `~/.cmux/bin/cmux` wrapper (also tries `/usr/local/bin/cmux`) so `cmux` is available in PATH on the remote.

### 3.5 CLI Relay (Running cmux Commands From Remote)
- `DONE` `cmuxd-remote` includes a table-driven CLI relay (`cli` subcommand) that maps CLI args to v1 text or v2 JSON-RPC messages.
- `DONE` busybox-style argv[0] detection: when invoked as `cmux` via wrapper/symlink, auto-dispatches to CLI relay.
- `DONE` background `ssh -N -R 127.0.0.1:PORT:127.0.0.1:LOCAL_RELAY_PORT` process reverse-forwards a TCP port to a dedicated authenticated local relay server. Uses TCP instead of Unix socket forwarding because many servers have `AllowStreamLocalForwarding` disabled.
- `DONE` relay process uses `-S none` / standalone SSH transport (avoids ControlMaster multiplexing and inherited `RemoteForward` directives) and `ExitOnForwardFailure=yes` so dead reverse binds fail fast instead of publishing bad relay metadata.
- `DONE` relay address written to `~/.cmux/socket_addr` on the remote only after the reverse forward survives startup validation.
- `DONE` Go CLI no longer polls for relay readiness. It dials the published relay once and only refreshes `~/.cmux/socket_addr` a single time to recover from a stale shared address rewrite.
- `DONE` `cmux ssh` startup exports session-local `CMUX_SOCKET_PATH=127.0.0.1:<relay_port>` so parallel sessions pin to their own relay instead of racing on shared socket_addr.
- `DONE` relay startup writes `~/.cmux/relay/<relay_port>.daemon_path`; remote `cmux` wrapper uses this to select the right daemon binary per session, including mixed local cmux versions.
- `DONE` relay startup writes `~/.cmux/relay/<relay_port>.auth` with a relay ID and token; the local relay requires HMAC-SHA256 challenge-response before forwarding any command to the real local socket.
- `DONE` ephemeral port range (49152-65535) filtered from probe results to exclude relay ports from other workspaces.
- `DONE` multi-workspace port conflict detection uses TCP connect check (`isLoopbackPortReachable`) so ports already forwarded by another workspace are silently skipped instead of flagged as conflicts.
- `DONE` orphaned relay SSH processes from previous app sessions are cleaned up before starting a new relay.

### 3.6 Artifact Trust
- `DONE` release and nightly workflows publish `cmuxd-remote` assets for `darwin/linux × arm64/amd64`.
- `DONE` release and nightly apps embed a compact `CMUXRemoteDaemonManifestJSON` in `Info.plist` with exact asset URLs and SHA-256 digests.
- `DONE` `cmux remote-daemon-status` exposes the current manifest entry, local cache verification state, release download command, and GitHub attestation verification command.

### 3.3 Error Surfacing
- `DONE` remote errors are surfaced in sidebar status + logs + notifications.
- `DONE` reconnect retry count/time is included in surfaced error text (for example, `retry 1 in 4s`).

### 3.4 Removed Temporary Behavior
- `DONE` removed remote listening-port probe loop and per-port SSH `-L` mirroring.
- `DONE` remote browser routing now uses a single shared local proxy endpoint instead of detected-port mirroring.
- `DONE` remote status now includes structured proxy metadata (`remote.proxy`) and `proxy_unavailable` error code when proxy setup fails.

## 4. Target Architecture (No Port Mirroring)

### 4.1 Browser Networking Path
1. `DONE` one local proxy endpoint is created per SSH transport/session key (not per detected port).
2. `DONE` endpoint is provided by a local broker that supports SOCKS5 + HTTP CONNECT and tunnels via daemon stream RPC.
3. `DONE` browser panels in remote workspaces are auto-wired to the workspace proxy endpoint.
4. `DONE` browser panels in local workspaces are not force-proxied.
5. `DONE` identical SSH transports share one endpoint via a transport-scoped broker.

### 4.2 WKWebView Wiring
1. `DONE` use workspace-scoped `WKWebsiteDataStore(forIdentifier:)`.
2. `DONE` apply workspace/browser scoped `proxyConfigurations`.
3. `DONE` prefer SOCKS5 proxy config.
4. `DONE` keep HTTP CONNECT proxy config as fallback.
5. `DONE` re-apply proxy config on reconnect/state updates.

### 4.3 Remote Daemon + Transport
1. `DONE` `cmuxd-remote` now supports proxy stream RPC (`proxy.open`, `proxy.close`, `proxy.write`, `proxy.stream.subscribe`) with pushed `proxy.stream.data/eof/error` events.
2. `DONE` local side now runs a shared local broker that serves SOCKS5/CONNECT and tunnels each stream over persistent daemon stdio RPC without polling reads.
3. `DONE` removed remote service-port discovery/probing from browser routing path.

### 4.4 Explicit Non-Goal
1. Automatic mirroring of every remote listening port to local loopback is not a goal for browser support.

## 5. PTY Resize Semantics (tmux-style)

### 5.1 Core Rule
For each session with multiple attachments, the effective PTY size is:
1. `cols = min(cols_i over attached clients)`
2. `rows = min(rows_i over attached clients)`

This is the `smallest screen wins` rule.

### 5.2 State Model
Per session track:
1. set of active attachments `{attachment_id -> cols, rows, updated_at}`
2. effective size currently applied to PTY
3. last-known size when temporarily unattached

### 5.3 Recompute Triggers
Recompute effective size on:
1. attachment create
2. attachment detach
3. resize event from any attachment
4. reconnect reattach

### 5.4 Correctness Requirements
1. Never shrink history because of UI relayout noise; only PTY viewport changes.
2. On reconnect, reuse persisted session and recompute from active attachments.
3. If no attachments remain, keep last-known PTY size (do not force 80x24 reset).

## 6. Milestones (Living Status)

| ID | Milestone | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| M-001 | `cmux ssh` workspace creation + metadata + optional `--name` | DONE | Covered by `tests_v2/test_ssh_remote_cli_metadata.py` |
| M-002 | Remote bootstrap/upload/start + hello handshake | DONE | Includes daemon capability handshake + status surfacing |
| M-003 | Reconnect/disconnect UX + API + improved error surfacing | DONE | Includes retry count in surfaced errors |
| M-004 | Docker e2e for bootstrap/reconnect shell niceties | DONE | Docker suites validate proxy-path bootstrap and reconnect behavior |
| M-004b | CLI relay: run cmux commands from within SSH sessions | DONE | Reverse TCP forward + Go CLI relay + bootstrap wrapper |
| M-005 | Remove automatic remote port mirroring path | DONE | `WorkspaceRemoteSessionController` now uses one shared daemon-backed proxy endpoint |
| M-006 | Transport-scoped local proxy broker (SOCKS5 + CONNECT) | DONE | Identical SSH transports now reuse one local proxy endpoint |
| M-007 | Remote proxy stream RPC in `cmuxd-remote` | DONE | `proxy.open/close/write/proxy.stream.subscribe` plus pushed stream events implemented |
| M-008 | WebView proxy auto-wiring for remote workspaces | DONE | Workspace-scoped `WKWebsiteDataStore.proxyConfigurations` wiring is active |
| M-009 | PTY resize coordinator (`smallest screen wins`) | DONE | Daemon session RPC now tracks attachments and applies min cols/rows semantics with unit tests |
| M-010 | Resize + proxy reconnect e2e test suites | DONE | `tests_v2/test_ssh_remote_docker_forwarding.py` validates HTTP/websocket egress plus SOCKS pipelined-payload handling; `tests_v2/test_ssh_remote_docker_reconnect.py` verifies reconnect recovery and repeats SOCKS pipelined-payload checks after host restart; `tests_v2/test_ssh_remote_proxy_bind_conflict.py` validates structured `proxy_unavailable` bind-conflict surfacing and `local_proxy_port` status retention under bind conflict; `tests_v2/test_ssh_remote_daemon_resize_stdio.py` validates session resize semantics over real stdio RPC process boundaries; `tests_v2/test_ssh_remote_cli_metadata.py` validates `workspace.remote.configure` numeric-string compatibility, explicit `null` clear semantics (including `workspace.remote.status` reflection), strict `port`/`local_proxy_port` validation (bounds/type), case-insensitive SSH option override precedence for StrictHostKeyChecking/control-socket keys, and `local_proxy_port` payload echo for deterministic bind-conflict test hook behavior |

## 7. Acceptance Test Matrix (With Status)

### 7.1 Terminal + Reconnect

| ID | Scenario | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T-001 | baseline remote connect | DONE |
| T-002 | identical host reuse semantics | DONE |
| T-003 | no `--name` | DONE |
| T-004 | reconnect API success/error paths | DONE |
| T-005 | retry count visible in daemon error detail | DONE |

### 7.2 CLI Relay

| ID | Scenario | Status |
|---|---|---|
| C-001 | `cmux ping` from remote session | DONE |
| C-002 | `cmux list-workspaces --json` from remote | DONE |
| C-003 | `cmux new-workspace` from remote | DONE |
| C-004 | `cmux rpc system.capabilities` passthrough | DONE |
| C-005 | TCP retry handles relay not yet established | DONE |
| C-006 | multi-workspace port conflict silent skip | DONE |
| C-007 | ephemeral port filtering excludes relay ports | DONE |

### 7.3 Browser Proxy (Target)

| ID | Scenario | Status |
|---|---|---|
| W-001 | remote workspace browser auto-proxied | DONE |
| W-002 | browser egress equals remote network path | DONE |
| W-003 | websocket via SOCKS5/CONNECT through remote daemon | DONE |
| W-004 | reconnect restores browser proxy path automatically | DONE |
| W-005 | local proxy bind conflict yields structured `proxy_unavailable` | DONE |
| W-006 | proxy transport failure triggers daemon re-bootstrap and recovers after host recreation | DONE |
| W-007 | SOCKS greeting/connect + immediate pipelined payload in same write remains intact | DONE |

### 7.4 Resize

| ID | Scenario | Status |
|---|---|---|
| RZ-001 | two attachments, smallest wins | DONE |
| RZ-002 | grow one attachment, PTY stays bounded by smallest | DONE |
| RZ-003 | detach smallest, PTY expands to next smallest | DONE |
| RZ-004 | reconnect preserves session + applies recomputed size | DONE |
| RZ-005 | daemon stdio RPC round-trip enforces resize semantics end-to-end | DONE |

## 8. Removal Checklist (Port Mirroring)

Before declaring browser proxying complete:
1. `DONE` remove remote port probe loop and `-L` auto-forward orchestration
2. `DONE` remove mirror-specific routing behavior as default remote behavior
3. `DONE` replace mirroring docker assertions with proxy egress assertions
4. `DONE` keep optional explicit user-driven forwarding out of this path; no automatic mirroring remains in browser routing

## 9. Open Decisions

1. Proxy auth policy for local broker (`none` vs optional credentials).
2. Reconnect backoff profile and max retry budget.

## 10. Socket API Contract Notes

### 10.1 `workspace.remote.configure` Port Fields
1. `port` and `local_proxy_port` accept integer values and numeric strings.
2. Explicit `null` clears each field.
3. Out-of-range values and invalid types (for example booleans/non-numeric strings/fractional numbers) return `invalid_params`.
4. `local_proxy_port` is an internal deterministic test hook to force local bind conflicts in regression coverage.

### 10.2 SSH Option Precedence
1. `StrictHostKeyChecking` default (`accept-new`) is only injected when no user override is present.
2. Control-socket defaults (`ControlMaster`, `ControlPersist`, `ControlPath`) are only injected when missing.
3. SSH option key matching is case-insensitive for precedence checks in both CLI-built commands and remote configure payloads.

### 10.3 SSH Docker E2E Harness Knobs
1. `CMUX_SSH_TEST_DOCKER_HOST` sets the SSH destination host/IP used by docker-backed SSH fixtures (default `127.0.0.1`).
2. `CMUX_SSH_TEST_DOCKER_BIND_ADDR` sets the bind address used in fixture container publish mappings (default `127.0.0.1`).
3. Defaults preserve loopback behavior on a single host; override both when docker runs on a different host (for example VM -> host OrbStack).

content.content:
# V2 Socket API Migration Overview

## Core Protocol Structure

The v2 JSON socket protocol uses newline-delimited JSON for both requests and responses:

**Request format:**
```json
{"id":"1","method":"workspace.list","params":{}}
```

**Success response:**
```json
{"id":"1","ok":true,"result":{...}}
```

**Error response:**
```json
{"id":"1","ok":false,"error":{"code":"not_found","message":"workspace not found"}}
```

The `id` field echoes back as either a string or number, serving as the stable request identifier.

## Key Design Principles

The migration maintains backward compatibility: "Keep **v1 fully working** until v2 reaches feature parity." Responses may include ephemeral `index` fields for debugging, but **IDs serve as stable handles** for surfaces, workspaces, panes, and windows.

## Complete Method Mapping

**Windows:** `window.list`, `window.current`, `window.focus`, `window.create`, `window.close`, `workspace.move_to_window`

**Workspaces:** `workspace.list`, `workspace.create`, `workspace.select`, `workspace.current`, `workspace.close`

**Surfaces/Splits:** `surface.list`, `surface.focus`, `surface.split`, `surface.create`, `surface.close`, `surface.drag_to_split`, `surface.refresh`, `surface.health`, `surface.trigger_flash`

**Panes:** `pane.list`, `pane.focus`, `pane.surfaces`, `pane.create`

**Input:** `surface.send_text`, `surface.send_key`

**Notifications:** `notification.create`, `notification.create_for_surface`, `notification.create_for_target`, `notification.list`, `notification.clear`

**Browser:** `browser.open_split`, `browser.navigate`, `browser.back`, `browser.forward`, `browser.reload`, `browser.url.get`, `browser.focus_webview`, `browser.is_webview_focused`

**Debug/Test:** `debug.shortcut.set`, `debug.shortcut.simulate`, `debug.type`, `debug.app.activate`, `debug.terminal.*`, `debug.layout`, `debug.bonsplit_underflow.*`, `debug.empty_panel.*`, `debug.notification.focus`, `debug.flash.*`, `debug.panel_snapshot.*`, `debug.window.screenshot`

## Testing Strategy

Both test suites run independently: v1 tests in `tests/`, v2 tests in `tests_v2/` using a dedicated JSON client.

# PROJECTS.md Summary: cmux Development Tracking

## Overview
This document tracks cross-project features, bugs, and backlog items for **cmux**, a terminal multiplexer with browser integration built on macOS.

## Key Technical Areas from "Done" Section

### Socket API & CLI
The project implements a **v2 JSON socket API** (handle-based) alongside v1 compatibility. Recent completions include:

- "Extended the socket/CLI to handle multi-window automation: added v2 `window.list/current/focus/create/close`, v2 `workspace.move_to_window`"
- Expanded CLI coverage with commands like `list-panes`, `focus-pane`, `new-pane`, `new-surface`, `close-surface`, `drag-surface-to-split`
- CLI discovery commands: `cmux identify` and `cmux capabilities` map to `system.identify` and `system.capabilities`

### Browser Automation
Browser pane features include WKWebView integration with commands for address bar interaction, search engine configuration, and omnibar state management. The project adapted automation patterns from "vercel-labs/agent-browser" for cmux-native workflows.

### Testing & Regression Coverage
Extensive test suites across both v1 and v2 APIs:
- VM UI regression tests for split operations, notifications, and keyboard shortcuts
- Python-based test coverage (`tests_v2/test_*.py`) validating API parity

## Backlog Priorities

1. **WKWebView stability** – intermittent crash/relaunch investigation
2. **Automation Mode** – XCUITest expansion pending VM reliability
3. **API standardization** – split insertion semantics and pane selection across interfaces
4. **Agent-browser port spec** – command mapping, surface targeting, and v1 shim strategy

## Licensing
"Relicensed the repository to strong copyleft (`AGPL-3.0-or-later`)" on 2026-02-14.

# Agent-Browser Port Spec – Full Content

**Last updated:** February 13, 2026  
**Source:** `vercel-labs/agent-browser` @ `03a8cb9`

## Goals

The port aims to deliver an LLM-friendly browser automation API in cmux with stable handles, maintaining backward compatibility while v2 reaches feature parity. Key objectives include porting the command surface meaningfully for `WKWebView`, preserving `surface_id` identity across move/reorder operations, and ensuring both v1 and v2 test suites pass before deprecation.

## Validation Status (February 12, 2026)

Both test suites pass on `cmux-vm`. Browser parity suites now include `test_browser_api_comprehensive.py`, `test_browser_api_p0.py`, `test_browser_api_extended_families.py`, `test_browser_api_unsupported_matrix.py`, and `test_browser_cli_agent_port.py`. Visual tests report D12 (Nested: Close Top of T-shape) as a known non-blocking VM issue.

## Core Concepts

Public APIs standardize on `surface` (tab within a pane) and `pane` (split region). The canonical "where am I?" call is `system.identify`, which must return `focused.window_id`, `focused.workspace_id`, `focused.pane_id`, and `focused.surface_id`. Browser workflows benefit from extended fields like `focused.surface_type`, `focused.browser.url`, and `focused.browser.title`.

## Agent-Browser Command Inventory

The spec lists 49 top-level CLI verbs and 16 subcommand families, totaling 125 protocol actions. Of these, 93 are directly emitted by the CLI parser; 32 exist protocol-only (including `addinitscript`, `addscript`, `clipboard`, `dispatch`, `evalhandle`, `har_start`, `screencast_start`, and others).

## cmux Target API (v2)

Already present: `system.ping`, `system.capabilities`, `system.identify`, window/workspace/pane/surface management, and basic browser navigation.

**P0 (core parity):** snapshot, eval, wait, click/dblclick, type/fill, press/keydown/keyup, hover/focus, check/uncheck, select, scroll, getters (url/title/text/html/value/attr/count/box/styles), state checks (visible/enabled/checked), screenshot, and webview focus control.

**P1 (important):** locators (role/text/label/placeholder/alt/title/testid), frame switching, dialog handling, download waiting, console/error retrieval, highlight, state persistence.

**P2 (advanced/optional):** network interception, emulation settings, trace/video/screencast, script injection, raw input injection.

## CLI Specification

Primary usage:
```
cmux browser --surface <surface-id> <agent-browser-style-command...>
cmux browser <surface-id> <agent-browser-style-command...>
```

Agent discovery via `cmux identify` and `cmux browser identify --surface <surface-id>`. Compatibility preserved with `--panel` as an alias for `--surface`.

## Move/Reorder Specification

Required capabilities include reordering surfaces within panes, moving surfaces between panes/workspaces/windows, and reordering workspaces. Proposed methods: `surface.move`, `surface.reorder`, and `workspace.reorder`. Hard invariant: `surface_id` remains unchanged after all operations.

## Comprehensive TODO Status

Phases 0–3 marked complete (contract/routing, P0 core methods, P1 locators/session methods, P2 move/reorder). Phase 4 (advanced parity) is in progress; Phase 5 (compatibility/migration) awaits final documentation; Phase 6 (docs/examples) pending.

## Test Port Plan

Port targets include `src/browser.test.ts`, `src/actions.test.ts`, and `src/protocol.test.ts`. Six cmux browser test suites now implemented. Migration gates require new tests to pass, v2 regression suites to succeed, v1 suites to work with shim, and no regressions in existing workflows.

## Decision Log (Locked February 12, 2026)

Key settled decisions: `cmux browser tab` maps to browser surface tabs only; default placement is caller-relative; network parity is full parity; output uses short refs by default (`surface:N`, `pane:N`, etc.); browser create/move commands expose placement metadata; `browser fill` treats empty text as clear; mutating actions can return post-action snapshots via `snapshot_after`.

## Remaining Open Decisions

1. Unsupported command policy: strict `not_supported` errors versus best-effort fallback.
2. Protocol-only agent-browser action exposure timing in public release.

# cmux Contributing Guide Summary

The **cmux** repository is a macOS terminal multiplexer project hosted at `manaflow-ai/cmux`. Here are the key contribution details:

## Prerequisites
- macOS 14+
- Xcode 15+
- Zig programming language

## Setup Process
Contributors must "clone the repository with submodules" and "run the setup script" (`./scripts/setup.sh`), which initializes git submodules and builds the GhosttyKit.xcframework.

## Development Workflow
The project provides several helper scripts:
- `reload.sh` - builds and launches the Debug app
- `reloadp.sh` - builds and launches the Release version
- `rebuild.sh` - performs a clean rebuild

## Ghostty Submodule Management
The project uses a fork of Ghostty (`manaflow-ai/ghostty`). Contributors working on ghostty changes must rebuild the xcframework using Zig, then update the parent repository with submodule commits.

## Testing
The project includes both basic tests and UI tests that "run on VM" environments using SSH and xcodebuild commands.

## License Terms
Contributors agree their work is licensed under "AGPL-3.0-or-later" and grant Manaflow, Inc. "a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, irrevocable license" to use contributions under any license model.

# Ghostty Fork Changes Summary

This document outlines patches maintained in the manaflow-ai/ghostty fork, which builds on upstream v1.3.0 plus newer commits as of March 12, 2026.

## Key Modifications

The fork includes seven primary feature sets:

1. **OSC 99 Parser**: Adds kitty notification support to the terminal's OSC dispatcher
2. **macOS Display Link**: Restarts vsync callbacks when display changes occur
3. **Copy Mode API**: Restores keyboard selection functions for cmux compatibility
4. **Resize Handling**: Replays rendered frames during window resizing to eliminate visual artifacts
5. **Zsh Prompt Markers**: Distinguishes between new prompts (OSC 133;A) and redraws (OSC 133;P)
6. **Multiline Prompts**: Handles Pure-style prompts with proper marker placement
7. **Theme Picker**: Adds CLI helper integration for cmux theme previewing

## Important Rebase Considerations

When updating the fork, watch for conflicts in:
- `src/terminal/osc/parsers.zig` — preserve custom kitty_notification import
- `src/terminal/osc.zig` — verify OSC 99 dispatch integration
- `src/shell-integration/zsh/ghostty-integration` — maintain prompt marker logic
- `src/cli/list_themes.zig` — keep cmux environment-variable hooks functional

One prior patch (cursor-click-to-move respecting OSC 133) has been upstreamed and is no longer maintained locally.

# SocketControlSettings.swift - Socket Configuration Overview

This Swift file defines socket control modes and password management for the cmux terminal application. Here are the key details:

## Socket Control Modes

The file defines five security modes via the `SocketControlMode` enum:

- **Off**: Disables the local control socket entirely
- **cmuxOnly**: "Only processes started inside cmux terminals can send commands"
- **Automation**: Permits external automation clients from the macOS user without ancestry verification
- **Password**: "Require socket authentication with a password stored in a local file"
- **AllowAll**: Full open access with no authentication (marked unsafe)

## Default Socket Paths

The socket path resolution follows this hierarchy:

1. **Tagged debug sockets**: `/tmp/cmux-debug-{tag}.sock` for debug builds with launch tags
2. **Debug variants**: `/tmp/cmux-debug.sock` or `/tmp/cmux-nightly.sock` 
3. **Stable default**: User-scoped path in application support directory
4. **Legacy fallback**: `/tmp/cmux.sock`

The stable path uses `~/Library/Application Support/cmux/cmux.sock` when accessible.

## Password Management

The `SocketControlPasswordStore` enum handles credentials through multiple sources in priority order:

- Environment variable `CMUX_SOCKET_PASSWORD`
- File-based storage in application support directory
- Legacy macOS Keychain (with automatic migration to file storage)

The password file is stored at `~/Library/Application Support/cmux/socket-control-password` with restricted permissions (`0o600`).

## Configuration Override

The `SocketControlSettings` struct provides environment-based overrides via `CMUX_SOCKET_MODE` and `CMUX_SOCKET_ENABLE` variables, enabling runtime mode changes without restarting the application.

I now have comprehensive information. Here is the full breakdown:

---

## cmux — Full Repository Documentation Summary

### 1. Full README Content

**cmux** is a native macOS terminal application (Swift/AppKit) built on top of the Ghostty terminal emulator. It is designed specifically for AI coding agents.

**Core Features:**
- **Notification rings**: Panes get a blue ring and tabs highlight when coding agents need your attention. A notification panel shows all pending alerts.
- **In-app browser**: A scriptable API ported from `vercel-labs/agent-browser` for agent interaction with web UIs.
- **Vertical + horizontal tabs**: Sidebar shows git branch, linked PR status/number, working directory, listening ports, and latest notification text.
- **Native performance**: Built with Swift and AppKit (not Electron), GPU-accelerated via libghostty.
- **Ghostty compatible**: Reads `~/.config/ghostty/config` for themes and fonts.
- **Auto-updates** via Sparkle.

**Installation:**
```bash
brew tap manaflow-ai/cmux
brew install --cask cmux
```
Or download a DMG.

**Philosophy:** "cmux is not prescriptive about how developers hold their tools" — it is "a primitive, not a solution."

- `remote-daemon-spec.md`
- `socket-focus-steal-audit.todo.md`

- `assets/`

---

### 3. CLI Documentation / Command Reference

The CLI is implemented in `CLI/cmux.swift`. It communicates with the running cmux app via a Unix domain socket.

**Command families:**

| Category | Commands |
|---|---|
| System | `ping`, `identify`, `capabilities` |
| Notifications | `notify --title --subtitle --body --tab --panel`, `list-notifications`, `clear-notifications` |
| Windows | `list-windows`, `focus-window`, `create-window`, `close-window` |
| Workspaces | `list-workspaces`, `new-workspace`, `select-workspace`, `rename-workspace`, `close-workspace` |
| Panes | `list-panes`, `focus-pane`, `new-pane` |
| Surfaces/Splits | `new-surface`, `close-surface`, `focus-surface`, `split-surface`, `drag-surface-to-split` |
| Input | `send-text`, `send-keys` |
| Browser | `browser navigate`, `browser reload`, `browser back`, `browser forward`, `browser url`, `browser focus`, `browser snapshot`, `browser eval`, `browser click`, `browser type`, `browser fill`, `browser press`, etc. |
| SSH | `ssh`, `remote-daemon-status` |
| RPC passthrough | `rpc <method> [params]` |

**Notify usage:**
```bash
cmux notify --title "Build Complete"
cmux notify --title "Claude Code" --subtitle "Permission" --body "Approval needed"
cmux notify --title "Done" --tab 0 --panel 1
```

**Browser usage:**
```bash
cmux browser --surface <surface-id> <agent-browser-command>
cmux browser <surface-id> snapshot
cmux browser <surface-id> eval "document.title"
cmux browser <surface-id> click "#button"
cmux browser <surface-id> type "input" "hello"
```

**Environment variables set by cmux in child shells:**

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `CMUX_SOCKET_PATH` | Path to the control socket |
| `CMUX_TAB_ID` | UUID of the current tab |
| `CMUX_PANEL_ID` | UUID of the current panel |

---

### 4. Unix Socket API

#### Socket Path Resolution (priority order)
1. Tagged debug: `/tmp/cmux-debug-<tag>.sock`
2. Debug: `/tmp/cmux-debug.sock` or `/tmp/cmux-nightly.sock`
3. Stable default: `~/Library/Application Support/cmux/cmux.sock`
4. Legacy fallback: `/tmp/cmux.sock`

Override via `CMUX_SOCKET_PATH` environment variable.

#### Security Modes (`SocketControlMode`)
- **Off** — socket disabled
- **cmuxOnly** — only processes started inside cmux terminals can send commands (ancestry check)
- **Automation** — external automation clients from the macOS user, no ancestry check
- **Password** — requires HMAC-SHA256 authentication; password stored in `~/Library/Application Support/cmux/socket-control-password` (mode 0600); also accepts `CMUX_SOCKET_PASSWORD` env var or Keychain
- **AllowAll** — no authentication (unsafe)

Override mode at runtime via `CMUX_SOCKET_MODE` or `CMUX_SOCKET_ENABLE`.

#### V1 Protocol (text-based)
Legacy line-delimited text commands. Still fully supported.

#### V2 Protocol (JSON-RPC, newline-delimited)

**Request:**
```json
{"id":"1","method":"workspace.list","params":{}}
```

**Success response:**
```json
{"id":"1","ok":true,"result":{...}}
```

**Error response:**
```json
{"id":"1","ok":false,"error":{"code":"not_found","message":"workspace not found"}}
```

The `id` field is echoed back as a stable request identifier. IDs (not indexes) are the stable handles for surfaces, workspaces, panes, and windows.

**Full v2 method list:**

- **Windows:** `window.list`, `window.current`, `window.focus`, `window.create`, `window.close`, `workspace.move_to_window`
- **Workspaces:** `workspace.list`, `workspace.create`, `workspace.select`, `workspace.current`, `workspace.close`
- **Surfaces/Splits:** `surface.list`, `surface.focus`, `surface.split`, `surface.create`, `surface.close`, `surface.drag_to_split`, `surface.refresh`, `surface.health`, `surface.trigger_flash`
- **Panes:** `pane.list`, `pane.focus`, `pane.surfaces`, `pane.create`
- **Input:** `surface.send_text`, `surface.send_key`
- **Notifications:** `notification.create`, `notification.create_for_surface`, `notification.create_for_target`, `notification.list`, `notification.clear`
- **Browser:** `browser.open_split`, `browser.navigate`, `browser.back`, `browser.forward`, `browser.reload`, `browser.url.get`, `browser.focus_webview`, `browser.is_webview_focused`
- **System:** `system.ping`, `system.capabilities`, `system.identify`
- **Remote/SSH:** `workspace.remote.reconnect`, `workspace.remote.configure`, `workspace.remote.status`
- **Debug/Test:** `debug.shortcut.set`, `debug.shortcut.simulate`, `debug.type`, `debug.app.activate`, `debug.terminal.*`, `debug.layout`, `debug.flash.*`, `debug.window.screenshot`, `debug.panel_snapshot.*`, etc.

**`system.identify` response includes:** `focused.window_id`, `focused.workspace_id`, `focused.pane_id`, `focused.surface_id`, `focused.surface_type`, `focused.browser.url`, `focused.browser.title`

**Remote daemon RPC methods** (over stdio to `cmuxd-remote`):
- `proxy.open`, `proxy.close`, `proxy.write`, `proxy.stream.subscribe`
- `session.open`, `session.attach`, `session.resize`, `session.detach`, `session.status`, `session.close`
- Pushed events: `proxy.stream.data`, `proxy.stream.eof`, `proxy.stream.error`

---

### 5. Hook System

cmux integrates with agent hook systems via the `cmux notify` CLI command and terminal OSC sequences.

#### OSC Sequences Supported
- **OSC 9** — desktop notifications
- **OSC 99** — Kitty notification protocol (custom fork patch)
- **OSC 133** — shell integration prompt markers (A = new prompt, P = redraw)
- **OSC 777** — notification protocol

#### Claude Code Hooks (`~/.claude/settings.json`)
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "Notification": [
      {
        "matcher": "idle_prompt",
        "hooks": [{
          "type": "command",
          "command": "command -v cmux &>/dev/null && cmux notify --title 'Claude Code' --body 'Waiting for input' || osascript -e 'display notification \"Waiting for input\" with title \"Claude Code\"'"
        }]
      },
      {
        "matcher": "permission_prompt",
        "hooks": [{
          "type": "command",
          "command": "command -v cmux &>/dev/null && cmux notify --title 'Claude Code' --subtitle 'Permission' --body 'Approval needed' || osascript -e 'display notification \"Approval needed\" with title \"Claude Code\"'"
        }]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

#### OpenAI Codex (`~/.codex/config.toml`)
```toml
notify = ["bash", "-c", "command -v cmux &>/dev/null && cmux notify --title Codex --body \"$(echo $1 | jq -r '.\"last-assistant-message\" // \"Turn complete\"' 2>/dev/null | head -c 100)\" || osascript -e 'display notification \"Turn complete\" with title \"Codex\"'", "--"]
```

#### OpenCode Plugin (`.opencode/plugins/cmux-notify.js`)
```javascript
export const CmuxNotificationPlugin = async ({ $ }) => {
  const notify = async (title, body) => {
    try {
      await $`command -v cmux && cmux notify --title ${title} --body ${body}`;
    } catch {
      await $`osascript -e ${"display notification \"" + body + "\" with title \"" + title + "\""}`;
    }
  };
  return {
    event: async ({ event }) => {
      if (event.type === "session.idle") {
        await notify("OpenCode", "Session idle");
      }
    },
  };
};
```

**Best practices:**
1. Always guard with `command -v cmux` before calling
2. Provide `|| osascript` fallback for non-cmux environments
3. Keep titles brief

---

### 6. MCP Server

A search of the repository for "mcp" returned **0 results**. cmux does not implement or expose an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. The project integrates with AI agents (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, etc.) exclusively through:
- The Unix socket JSON-RPC API (directly or via the `cmux` CLI relay)
- Terminal OSC notification sequences
- Shell environment variables (`CMUX_SOCKET_PATH`, `CMUX_TAB_ID`, `CMUX_PANEL_ID`)
- Agent-specific hook/plugin systems (as documented above)

There is no MCP server in this repository as of the current state.

## cmux — Full Repository Documentation Summary

### 1. Full README Content

**cmux** is a native macOS terminal application (Swift/AppKit) built on top of the Ghostty terminal emulator. It is designed specifically for AI coding agents.

**Core Features:**
- **Notification rings**: Panes get a blue ring and tabs highlight when coding agents need your attention. A notification panel shows all pending alerts.
- **In-app browser**: A scriptable API ported from `vercel-labs/agent-browser` for agent interaction with web UIs.
- **Vertical + horizontal tabs**: Sidebar shows git branch, linked PR status/number, working directory, listening ports, and latest notification text.
- **Native performance**: Built with Swift and AppKit (not Electron), GPU-accelerated via libghostty.
- **Ghostty compatible**: Reads `~/.config/ghostty/config` for themes and fonts.
- **Auto-updates** via Sparkle.

**Installation:**
```bash
brew tap manaflow-ai/cmux
brew install --cask cmux
```
Or download a DMG.

**Philosophy:** "cmux is not prescriptive about how developers hold their tools" — it is "a primitive, not a solution."

**License:** AGPL-3.0-or-later; commercial licenses available via `founders@manaflow.com`.

content.text:
All 33 tests pass. Now here's the full feature gap analysis from superset.sh, cmux, and awesome-zellij — filtered to what's realistic for a Zellij-native CLI tool:

---

## Feature gaps

### High value, low effort (pure CLI additions)

| Feature | Source | What it means for dellij |
|---|---|---|
| **Import existing worktrees** | superset | `dellij import <path>` registers an existing worktree dir as a workspace without creating a new branch |
| **Open from existing branch** | superset | `dellij new --use-branch <name>` skips `git worktree add -b` and attaches to an existing branch |
| **`DELLIJ_*` env in workspace terminal** | superset/cmux | The generated KDL layout should inject env vars into pane shells, not just hook scripts |
| **Ahead/behind counts in `list`** | superset | `git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/<branch>` per workspace, show ↑N ↓N |
| **PR number in `list`/`show`** | superset | Store an optional `pr` field on `Workspace`; `dellij pr set <slug> <number>` |
| **`dellij new --base-pr <number>`** | superset | Create worktree from a PR's branch via `gh pr checkout` |

### Medium effort, significant UX lift

| Feature | Source | What it means for dellij |
|---|---|---|
| **Tab dedup / focus-if-open** | architecture gap | Plugin pipe: if tab `slug` exists, `switch_tab_to` instead of `new-tab` |
| **Attention indicators on tabs** | `zellij-attention`, zellaude | Plugin subscribes to `TabUpdate`; tabs with `error`/`blocked` status get a `!` glyph in the ribbon |
| **`dellij send <slug> <text>`** | cmux `send-text` | Send text to workspace's shell pane via `zellij pipe` → plugin → `write_chars()` |
| **Claude Code hook snippets** | cmux hooks | `dellij doctor` should print ready-to-paste hook configs for Claude Code/Codex/OpenCode that call `dellij status <slug> waiting` |
| **Multi-layout support** | cmux/superset | Config `settings.layouts` map; `dellij new --layout <name>` picks an alternative KDL template (e.g. 2-pane, agent-only) |
| **zoxide integration** | `zsm`, `zellij-sessionizer` | `dellij open` without a slug could fuzzy-search workspaces + zoxide entries |

### Longer-term / requires plugin pipe API

| Feature | Source | What it means for dellij |
|---|---|---|
| **`dellij read <slug>`** | cmux `read-screen` | Capture a pane's scrollback via plugin `read_pane_contents()` — useful for agent-to-agent orchestration |
| **Progress indicator** | cmux `set-progress` | Status enum extension + plugin renders a mini bar: `working [===   ]` based on a `0–100` field |
| **Notification delivery** | cmux, OSC 9 | When status → `blocked`/`error`, emit OSC 9 in the workspace pane header so terminal notifiers fire |

### Out of scope (GUI-only in competitors)

Built-in diff viewer, in-app browser, native sidebar, commit staging UI, IDE deep-linking — all require a GUI host that Zellij doesn't provide.

---

## awesome-zellij plugins worth integrating or referencing

- **[zellij-attention](https://github.com/zellij-org/awesome-zellij#zellij-attention)** — already solves tab-attention-indicators; dellij could adopt its pattern or make the status plugin emit compatible tab-name annotations
- **[zellij-worktree](https://github.com/zellij-org/awesome-zellij#zellij-worktree)** — a plugin that manages git worktrees; worth checking for overlap/inspiration
- **[multitask](https://github.com/zellij-org/awesome-zellij#multitask)** — mini-CI in a pane; dellij bookmarks could run via multitask for parallel task tracking
- **[zellij-send-keys](https://github.com/zellij-org/awesome-zellij#zellij-send-keys)** — already implements pane-targeting send; dellij's `dellij send` could be a thin wrapper
- **[zellij-playbooks](https://github.com/zellij-org/awesome-zellij#zellij-playbooks)** — YAML/TOML playbooks; dellij's bookmark system overlaps but is simpler

The two biggest bang-for-buck next steps remain **tab dedup via pipe API** and **Claude Code hook snippets in `doctor`** — the first removes the most visible daily friction, the second gives agents a zero-config way to update dellij status automatically.

input.prompt:
Design a comprehensive implementation plan for expanding the `dellij` project at `/home/diogenes/Projects/dellij`.

## Current state
- `crates/dellij/` — Rust CLI binary (910 lines, main.rs only)
- `plugin/` — Zellij WASM status plugin (220 lines)
- Uses shell calls to `zellij` CLI for orchestration
- Tracks git worktrees, workspace status, bookmarks

## Target: implement ALL of these features

### Tier 1 — CLI enhancements (pure Rust, no new deps beyond existing)
- `dellij import <path>` — register existing worktree
- `dellij new --use-branch <name>` — attach to existing branch
- `DELLIJ_*` env vars injected into the KDL layout pane shells (not just hooks)
- Ahead/behind commit counts in `dellij list`
- PR number field on workspace + `dellij pr set <slug> <number>`
- `dellij new --base-pr <number>` via `gh pr checkout`
- Multi-layout support: `settings.layouts` map, `dellij new --layout <name>`
- `dellij doctor` prints Claude Code / Codex / OpenCode hook config snippets

### Tier 2 — Zellij plugin pipe API (plugin becomes read-write controller)
- Plugin subscribes to PipeMessage events
- `zellij pipe` from CLI sends JSON to plugin for tab dedup/focus-if-open
- Attention indicators: plugin annotates tab names for error/blocked workspaces
- `dellij send <slug> <text>` — send text to workspace shell pane via plugin

### Tier 3 — GPUI desktop GUI (new crate: `crates/dellij-gui/`)
Using the GPUI framework (same framework as Zed editor: https://github.com/zed-industries/zed/tree/main/crates/gpui)

GUI features needed:
- **Sidebar** — list all workspaces with status dots, branch name, ports, PR number, ahead/behind
- **Diff viewer** — show `git diff` between workspace branch and base branch (can use Zed's diff UI patterns)
- **Status dashboard** — aggregate view of all workspace statuses
- **Notification panel** — list of workspaces needing attention (blocked/error)
- **IDE deep-linking** — buttons to open workspace in VS Code, Cursor, Zed
- **Browser pane** — embedded WebView using `wry` crate for web preview/automation

### Architecture decisions needed
1. Should `dellij-gui` be a separate binary or subcommand (`dellij ui`)?
2. How does the GUI stay in sync with CLI state? (file watching on `.dellij/status/*.json`?)
3. How does GPUI integrate with `wry` for the browser pane?
4. What crate structure makes sense? (`dellij-core` for shared types?)
5. How does the plugin pipe API work — what JSON protocol?

Read the current source files and design a full implementation plan with:
- New crate structure
- Which files to create/modify
- Order of implementation (what to do first)
- Key dependencies to add
- Any architectural risks

- **Sidebar** — list all workspaces with status dots, branch name, ports, PR number, ahead/behind
- **Diff viewer** — show `git diff` between workspace branch and base branch (can use Zed's diff UI patterns)
- **Status dashboard** — aggregate view of all workspace statuses
- **Notification panel** — list of workspaces needing attention (blocked/error)
- **IDE deep-linking** — buttons to open workspace in VS Code, Cursor, Zed
- **Browser pane** — embedded WebView using `wry` crate for web preview/automation

### Architecture decisions needed
1. Should `dellij-gui` be a separate binary or subcommand (`dellij ui`)?
2. How does the GUI stay in sync with CLI state? (file watching on `.dellij/status/*.json`?)
3. How does GPUI integrate with `wry` for the browser pane?
4. What crate structure makes sense? (`dellij-core` for shared types?)
5. How does the plugin pipe API work — what JSON protocol?

Read the current source files and design a full implementation plan with:
- New crate structure
- Which files to create/modify
- Order of implementation (what to do first)
- Key dependencies to add
- Any architectural risks

content.text:
Zero warnings, zero errors. Here's what was built:

---

## What was implemented

### New crate: `crates/dellij-core/`
Shared types and utilities extracted so both the CLI and GUI can use them without circular deps:
- All data types: `Workspace`, `Config`, `WorkspaceStatus`, `Bookmark`, `Settings`, `StatusFile`
- `WorkspaceStatus::needs_attention()` — true for `blocked | error | review`
- `AheadBehind` — computes and displays `↑N ↓N` via `git rev-list`
- `LayoutRenderer` — 4 built-in layouts: `default`, `minimal`, `full`, `agent-only`, plus user-defined KDL templates with `{cwd}`, `{agent_cmd}`, `{slug}`, `{branch}`, `{prompt}` placeholders
- `PipeCommand` — typed enum for the CLI↔plugin JSON protocol (`open`, `focus`, `send`, `status`)
- `workspace_diff()` — runs `git diff base...branch` for the diff viewer
- `open_in_editor()` — IDE deep-linking (code, cursor, zed, idea, etc.)

### CLI enhancements (`crates/dellij/`)
| New command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `dellij import <path>` | Register existing worktree, auto-detect branch |
| `dellij new --use-branch <name>` | Attach to existing branch instead of creating |
| `dellij new --base-pr <N>` | Create worktree from PR's branch via `gh` |
| `dellij new --layout <name>` | Use named layout (minimal/full/agent-only/custom) |
| `dellij send <slug> <text>` | Send text to workspace shell via plugin pipe |
| `dellij pr set/open/status` | Associate/view GitHub PR |
| `dellij edit <slug>` | Open worktree in VS Code/Cursor/Zed/`$EDITOR` |
| `dellij ui` | Launch `dellij-gui` binary |
| `dellij list` | Now shows `↑N ↓N`, `PR#N`, `!` attention marker |
| `dellij doctor` | Now prints Claude Code/Codex/OpenCode hook snippets |
- `DELLIJ_*` env vars now injected into **KDL layout pane shells** (not just hooks)
- Pipe-first: `open_workspace_tab` tries plugin pipe before shell fallback

### Plugin enhancements (`plugin/`)
- Subscribes to `PipeMessage` events — receives `open`, `focus`, `send`, `status` commands
- **Tab dedup/focus**: on `open`, checks if tab with matching slug exists → `go_to_tab()` vs `new_tabs_with_layout()`
- **Attention indicators**: `sync_tab_attention()` renames tabs to `!slug` when `blocked|error|review`
- **`send` command**: focuses the workspace tab then `write_chars()` to its shell pane
- Tracks **all tabs** (not just active), tracks pane IDs via `PaneUpdate`
- `StatusFile` now carries `pr_number` and `needs_attention` fields

### GPUI desktop app (`crates/dellij-gui/`)
- **Sidebar** — workspace list with status dot, branch, `↑N ↓N`, ports, `PR#N`, attention badge; click to select; IDE deep-link buttons (VS Code, Cursor, Zed); browser launch button
- **Diff viewer** — parses `git diff` output into `Added`/`Removed`/`Context`/`HunkHeader`/`FileHeader` lines, renders with green/red highlighting, line numbers, monospace font
- **Notification panel** — cards for all workspaces with `needs_attention()`, border-left colored by status, latest note, PR number
- **Browser pane** — `wry` WebView in its own OS thread/window with injected `window.dellij` JS API (`snapshot`, `click`, `fill`, `eval`)
- **File watcher** — `notify` crate watches `.dellij/status/` for live reload
- Catppuccin Mocha color palette throughout
